actions.thought: 52 offsuit is one of the worst starting hands in poker, and I'm in UTG (earliest position). This is a clear fold - no reason to play this trash hand from the worst position at the table.

Unopened pot in middle position with 100bb stacks. 32o has very poor equity, plays terribly postflop (dominated, low straight/flush potential), and doesn’t realize equity well when called. No need to invest chips this early without a clear edge; folding preserves stack and avoids negative-EV spots.

I am in the Cutoff position with Tc 8c (Ten-Eight suited) in a 6-handed game. The action has folded to me. This is a reasonable suited connector/gapper that plays well post-flop and allows me to apply pressure on the Button and Blinds. Opening from the Cutoff is standard with this holding to steal the blinds or play the pot in position against the blinds. A standard open size of 2.5x the big blind (250 chips) balances pot odds for opponents while building the pot with a playable hand.

Fold. Facing a cutoff open to 2.5x with 10♥2♥ on the button, the hand has very poor postflop playability and will be dominated a large portion of the time (lots of hands in the CO’s opening range have a Ten or higher or better kicker). Calling 250 into a 400 pot requires ~38.5% equity to break even right away — more than this hand will realise vs a standard CO range — and 3-betting as a bluff is too thin without reads (and puts me in a bad spot vs a possible 4-bet). With 100BB effective and no information yet, I’ll conserve chips and wait for a better, more playable spot in position.

Preflop in the small blind with T♦ 3♠, a weak offsuit hand with poor playability. Facing a cutoff raise to 250 (200 to call OOP), the pot odds are marginal (~2:1), but my hand has no equity realization potential, poor post-flop prospects out of position, and reverse implied odds if I hit. Standard fold to preserve stack early in tournament.

I am in the Big Blind holding Q4 offsuit. The Cutoff (Pro) raised to 2.5x (250). Although the pot odds are roughly 2.7 to 1 to make the call, Q4o is a very weak hand that plays poorly out of position. Defending such a wide and disconnected range against a standard open usually results in a negative expected value over time. Since it is the first hand of the tournament, I will play disciplined and fold this marginal hand.

KJo is strong enough to open from UTG in 6-max, especially 100bb deep. Raising builds the pot when ahead of blinds’ ranges, gives us initiative, and helps avoid going multiway. I’ll use a standard 2.5x open consistent with the table sizing we’ve seen.

I am holding Pocket Sixes (6c 6d) in Middle Position facing a standard 2.5bb open raise from the UTG player. With effective stacks around 100bb, I have excellent implied odds to call and try to flop a set ('set mining'). The Rule of 20 suggests I need roughly 5,000 in effective stacks to justify a 250 call, and we have nearly 10,000. Folding is too conservative, and 3-betting turns my hand into a bluff that isolates me against a stronger range. Calling keeps the pot manageable and allows me to evaluate the flop.

Facing an UTG open to 250 with a caller (Pro) already in, 4♠5♣ is too weak to continue. UTG’s range from early position is strong and my hand is easily dominated; playing multiway with a low offsuit connector has poor equity and realization. A cold 3-bet here would be a light bluff into callers and players left to act (button, blinds) and risks getting isolated or 4-bet — not a good spot early in the tournament with deep stacks. Pot odds don’t justify calling 250 to play a marginal hand multiway. I’ll fold and wait for a better position or a higher-quality hand to commit chips.

Q3o is a trash hand facing an UTG raise to 2.5BB and a flat call from MP. Position on button is nice but doesn't compensate for the poor equity against strong ranges. Pot odds are about 2.6:1 but my hand has very low equity multiway. Early in tournament with deep stacks, better to fold and wait for premium spots.

I am holding Jack-Ten offsuit in the small blind. An UTG player has raised to 2.5bb, and the middle position player has called. While the pot odds are somewhat attractive (200 to call into a 650 pot), JTo is a hand that is easily dominated by the UTG opening range (hands like AJ, KJ, QJ, or AT). Playing out of position for the rest of the hand against two opponents is disadvantageous. Given this is the early stage of the tournament, I prefer to fold marginal hands from the blinds to preserve my stack.

I have Kc4c in the BB facing a UTG raise to 250 and a MP call. Pot is 650 and I need 150 to call, giving me ~4.3:1 odds. K4 suited has decent playability - potential for flushes and top pair. The pot odds are good enough to call and see a flop, especially since I'm closing the action. Not strong enough to 3-bet.

Flopped top pair with weak kicker (K4) on Kd5hQh. OOP against two players who showed strength preflop. This board hits their raising ranges well (KQ, AK, QQ, KK). With a marginal hand, checking to see what they do is standard - pot control and gather information before committing more chips.

Multiway on a wet KQ5hh texture, KhJd is top pair but not a hand that wants to give a free card. There are many worse hands that can continue (Qx, 5x, smaller Kx, straight draws like JT/AT, and heart draws), and we want to deny equity rather than let two opponents realize for free. Since we’re not last to act (Pro still behind), I’ll use a solid value/protection sizing around ~55% pot and plan to proceed cautiously versus significant raises.

I called pre-flop with Pocket Sixes primarily to 'set mine'. The flop has come King-Queen-Five, which misses my hand completely and hits the UTG opener's range (AK, AQ, KQ) very hard. Facing a continuation bet of 450 into an 800 pot from the pre-flop aggressor, and with a player left to act behind me, my hand has insufficient equity to continue. I have only 2 outs to improve to a set, and even that might not be good. This is a standard 'fit or fold' situation.

I have top pair with weak kicker on Kd5hQh. FiveTwo opened UTG and is c-betting 450 into a pot of ~800. Pot odds are about 2.8:1 to call. While my kicker is weak, I still have top pair and position is now heads-up. FiveTwo's range includes AK/KQ that dominate me, but also flush draws, QJ, JTs, and some bluffs. Folding top pair here seems too tight, and raising would turn my hand into a bluff. Calling to see a turn and reassess is the best line.

The Ah on the turn is a terrible card for me. It completes heart flush draws, and any Ace now beats my top pair. FiveTwo's UTG range includes many Ax hands (AK, AQ, AJ) that now have me crushed, plus KQ still dominates me. Betting for value is problematic - better hands call/raise, worse hands fold. Checking to pot control and see what villain does is the correct play with this marginal holding on a scary runout.

Turn brings Ah, which is a big range-shifting card: it improves a lot of BB’s flop-calling range (Ax now top pair; some hands like JT can even make Broadway), and it also puts a third heart out there. With KhJd we’re now mostly a medium-strength showdown hand (pair of kings on an A-high board) plus a gutshot to Broadway (T) and some improvement outs (J/K). Betting risks getting check-raised off our equity and getting called mostly by better (Ax, two pair/sets, made straights) while folding out many worse Qx/5x that we already beat and that may bluff later. In position, the highest-EV line is to take the free card and re-evaluate rivers, bluff-catch on safe runouts, and improve when we hit.

River 3s changes nothing. I have top pair weak kicker. FiveTwo checked the turn after c-betting flop, suggesting either pot control or giving up. Board is scary with three hearts and an Ace. Betting for value is problematic - I get called/raised by better hands (flushes, AK, AQ, KQ, JT straights) and fold out worse. Check and hope to win at showdown with my marginal holding.

River completes the front-door hearts and the A high card is very favorable for BB’s flop-calling range (Ax, two pair like AQ/KQ, and made flushes). After Claude check-calls flop then goes check/check on turn and checks river, his range is polarized between medium showdown hands (some Kx, occasional Qx with a heart) and traps (flush/strong Ax). With KhJd we have a medium-strength showdown hand (pair of kings) that is hard to get called by worse often enough—most worse Qx will fold and many worse Kx will be cautious on this texture—while we risk running into a check-raise from strong hands. Taking the free showdown is highest EV.

I am in UTG position with 5h 7c. This is a very weak unsuited hand (7-high). Opening from early position requires a strong range because there are many players left to act who could wake up with a better hand or 3-bet. 5h7c is not playable from this position. I must fold.

I have A♠4♠ in middle with UTG folded and four players yet to act. With 100BB effective and a small pot (150), limping/calling would invite multiway action and leave me out of position; a standard open-raise is better to seize initiative and get fold equity. A4s is playable postflop (nut-flush and top-pair potential) so I’ll open to a normal 3x BB sizing to discourage callers but keep pots manageable. Plan: if I face a large 3-bet I’ll often fold; vs a single caller I’ll C-bet favorable textures and use position to extract value.

63d is a weak suited connector, not profitable to play out of position against a middle position raise to 300 (3x BB). Early in tournament with deep stacks (~100BB), pot odds of 1.5:1 are insufficient for speculative hand. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots.

I am holding Queen-Jack suited (Qh Jh) on the button, which is a very strong hand with high playability in position. Minni has opened for 3x the big blind from middle position. While 3-betting is a viable option to take control of the pot, calling allows me to play a pot in position with a hand that flops extremely well, while also keeping the pot size manageable early in the tournament. I will call the 300 to see the flop and use my positional advantage on later streets.
K3o is a weak hand, especially out of position. Facing a raise to 300 with a caller, I'm getting 3:1 but K3o plays poorly multiway and OOP. The kicker is terrible if I hit my king. Easy fold.

MP opens 3x and BTN flats, creating a strong squeeze spot from the BB with dead money already in (750). Ad8d has good properties for a light 3-bet: ace blocker reduces likelihood of AA/AK/AQ, and it retains playability when called (nut-flush potential, decent equity vs broadway-heavy ranges). Calling invites a dominated multiway spot OOP; squeezing pressures both ranges (BTN’s flat is often capped/price-sensitive) and can win the pot immediately or isolate with initiative. I’ll use a larger OOP squeeze sizing versus open+call and plan to fold to a 4-bet, then c-bet selectively on favorable textures if called.

I opened to 300 with A♠4♠. FiveTwo (BB) 3‑bet to 1300 and Flash (button) has already called my open, so calling will often put me OOP vs two players. A4s is a playable suited ace with nut‑flush potential and useful blockers; the 3‑bet is sizable but not so large that folding is mandatory. A min 4‑bet to 2300 as a bluff is unattractive here — it commits more chips out of position and is likely to be called by either the 3‑bettor or the button or face a 5‑bet shove. Folding is too tight given the hand’s postflop equity and the favorable pot odds to see a flop. I’ll call the 1000 more (total 1300) to see a flop; plan to c‑bet favorable textures heads‑up, play cautiously if it remains multiway, and fold to heavy action or a large 5‑bet shove.

I am holding Qh Jh on the button. After my initial call of Minni's open, FiveTwo in the big blind has put in a large squeeze to 1300, and Minni has called. I need to call 1000 more to see a flop in a pot that will be 3950. The pot odds are nearly 3:1, and I will have the absolute best position for the rest of the hand. While FiveTwo's squeeze range is likely strong, Qh Jh has excellent playability and implied odds in a multi-way 3-bet pot when deep-stacked. I will call to realize my equity and leverage my position post-flop.

Multiway as the preflop 3-bettor OOP, this 9-3-4 rainbow flop connects better with the callers’ ranges (small/medium pairs, suited connectors, 9x) than with my squeeze range. With Ad8d I mostly have ace-high with only a backdoor diamond draw and no meaningful straight equity, so a c-bet will get called a lot and rarely improves my equity realization. Checking keeps the pot controlled, allows me to realize my backdoor equity, and I can still respond appropriately if one of them stabs (including check-raising with strong value hands in my range on this texture).

I have A♠4♠ on 9c-3s-4d: middle pair (a 4) with the Ace blocker and a backdoor spade possibility. Preflop FiveTwo 3‑bet (strong range) and Flash called (wider). This is a multiway pot (pot = 3,950) and I'm effectively out of position to the button — betting here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and invite calls or raises from stronger hands that dominated me (9x, sets, overpairs, AK). There is limited fold equity betting into two players, and I prefer to control the pot size and realize equity. I’ll check and see a turn for free; if I improve (A or 4) I can extract value, and if I face large aggression on the turn I can fold without committing a big portion of my stack. Against small turn bets I’ll consider calls, but I won’t voluntarily build a big pot OOP with just middle pair.

The flop is 9c 3s 4d, which is very dry and disconnected. FiveTwo, who was the pre-flop aggressor with the squeeze, has checked, and Minni has also checked. While I have overcards (Qh Jh), I have missed the flop completely and have no direct draws. In a three-way bloated pot, bluffing here is risky because either opponent could be trapping with an overpair or holding a medium pocket pair that won't fold to a single bet. By checking behind, I take a free card to potentially improve on the turn or see if the action provides more information about their hand strengths without committing more chips with air.

Turn 6c further connects with the callers’ preflop ranges (66, 76s, 65s, 54s, 9x), while my specific hand (Ad8d) has essentially no real equity (no flush draw, no meaningful straight draw). In a 3-bet pot multiway, a delayed c-bet bluff needs both players to fold often, and this texture is one where they’ll continue a lot with pairs and draws—so stabbing is likely lighting chips on fire. I’ll check to keep the pot controlled, preserve the chance of a free river, and only continue later if I improve (A/8) or pick a very favorable river to bluff into capped ranges.

Board: 9c-3s-4d-6c. I hold A♠4♠ — a marginal made hand (pair of 4s, Ace kicker) in a three-way pot against a preflop 3-bettor (FiveTwo) and a caller (Flash). The pot is already large (3,950) and betting OOP into two players has very little fold equity; it would bloat the pot and commit a big chunk of my 8,700 stack while often being behind to 9x, sets or overpairs, or simply invited calls from draws/overcards. I prefer pot control: check the turn, call small bets to see the river if offered, but fold to substantial aggression. If checked through to the river I will re-evaluate — look for value if I improve or take a targeted bluff on a favorable river; no aggressive build here.

The turn card is the 6c, which doesn't significantly change the board texture. Both FiveTwo (the pre-flop aggressor) and Minni have checked for the second time. While their consecutive checks suggest weakness or a desire for pot control with marginal hands, I still have no made hand and no draws with Qh Jh. In a three-way pot of this size, bluffing is risky as someone may be planning to check-call with a medium pocket pair or an Ace-high hand. By checking behind, I take a free card to try and hit a Queen or Jack on the river, or simply reach a cheap showdown if the action remains checked around.

River comes Qs after three-way checks on flop/turn in a 3-bet pot. With Ad8d we have pure ace-high and no relevant blockers to the hands that will continue (Qx, 9x, 6x, pocket pairs). Multiway, a bluff has to get *both* players to fold, and given how easily they can arrive here with showdownable pairs that happily checked back twice for pot control, our fold equity isn’t high enough. Checking also preserves our chance to win at showdown versus busted A-high/overcard floats, which is a meaningful part of their checked-back ranges. So we take the free showdown and avoid torching chips.

Board: 9c 3s 4d 6c Qs. I hold A♠4♠ — only a single pair of 4s with an ace kicker. This is a marginal showdown hand in a three‑way pot (pot 3950) after a preflop 3‑bet and a call; the river Q is a scare card that improves many hands (Qx, 9x, 6x, sets, two‑pair). I have very little value to extract and minimal fold equity betting into two opponents out of position, so I’ll control the pot and check to showdown. If faced with a sizable river bet I’ll fold; only a small blocking bet from an opponent would merit a thin call based on reads.

I have hit top pair on the river with my Queen-Jack. The hand began with a pre-flop squeeze from FiveTwo and calls from Minni and myself, but the action has been checked through the flop and turn by all players. This sequence suggests that my opponents likely held overcards like AK or AJ that missed, or medium pocket pairs like JJ or TT that were playing cautiously. The Queen on the river is an excellent card for my range. Since both opponents have checked to me again, I will place a value bet. A bet of 1500 into the 3950 pot is sized to extract value from those medium pocket pairs or a pair of nines, while still being small enough to potentially look like a late-position bluff.

We’re facing a 1,500 bet into 5,450 in a 3-bet pot that went check/check/check on flop and turn. With Ad8d we have pure ace-high and essentially no relevant blockers: we don’t block Qx, 9x, 6x, or pocket pairs—the hands most likely to take this small river sizing for value. Even if Flash is stabbing sometimes, this is still multiway with Minni behind, who will continue very often with any pair that checked twice for pot control, which crushes our bluff-catch EV. Our hand is near the bottom of our range and doesn’t realize well in this spot, so we shouldn’t pay off.

Folding preserves stack and avoids getting dragged into a low-equity multiway river call.

I hold A♠4♠ on 9c-3s-4d-6c-Qs — a single pair (fours) with an Ace kicker. Pot is 5,450 and Flash (who called a preflop 3‑bet) fires a small 1,500 bet (~27% of the pot). His calling line preflop and passive line on earlier streets produce a fairly wide river range (Qx, 9x, 6x, small pairs, some Axs, and occasional bluffs). The bet size gives me ~21.6% break‑even equity (1500 / (5450+1500)), which is well within what my hand likely has versus a mixed value/bluff river range. Raising OOP here would bloat the pot with little fold equity and commit chips versus many hands that beat me, so the correct, lowest-variance play is to call the 1,500 to reach showdown. I will fold to substantial reraises/shoves but call this small river stab for value and to realize showdown equity.

Holding T3o (Ten of hearts, Three of clubs) UTG in a 6-player game is a very weak hand: unsuited, poorly connected, and heavily dominated by any Ten. From early position I should be playing a tight opening range. Calling the 100 to see a flop is speculative with little equity and poor post‑flop playability out of position; raising to 200 would be widening my range with a hand that performs poorly against likely callers and 3-bettors (players at this table have shown aggression). With a healthy 72bb stack I should conserve chips for stronger spots — fold preflop.

84o is a complete trash hand: offsuit, unconnected, low cards with minimal potential. In middle position after UTG fold, facing blinds who have shown some aggression in prior hands (e.g., FiveTwo 3betting). Deep stacked (99BB), no need to play speculative hands out of position early in tournament. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots.

I am in the Cutoff with Kh Js, which is a strong starting hand in a 6-max game. The action has folded to me, making this a standard opening situation. I will raise to 300 (3x the big blind) to take control of the pot and leverage my position and stack size against the button and the blinds.

I have A4o on the button facing a raise from the cutoff. While A4o is marginal, I have excellent position and can play this profitably post-flop. The raise is only 3x, making a call reasonable. Folding is too tight here on the button, and 3-betting with A4o is too aggressive without suited/connected strength. Calling to see a flop in position is the best play.

We’re in the small blind facing a CO open and a BTN flat, so we’ll be out of position in a likely multiway pot with the BB still to act. A4o has poor playability here: it’s frequently dominated when we make an ace, has no flush potential, and doesn’t realize equity well OOP. A squeeze bluff is unattractive given the apparent calling tendencies from Flash/Claude, so the highest-EV line is to avoid a marginal, dominated spot and preserve stack.

I am in the Big Blind with A3o facing a 3bb raise from the Cutoff and a call from the Button. While the direct pot odds are attractive (calling 200 to win a total pot of 950, approx 21%), the playability of A3o out of position in a multi-way pot is very poor. My equity realization will be low; if I hit an Ace, I am often dominated by the Cutoff's opening range or the Button's calling range, leading to significant reverse implied odds. Offsuit weak aces are generally folds in this specific multi-way configuration. Preserving my stack and avoiding difficult post-flop spots out of position is the prudent play here.

I have middle pair (Jacks) with a King kicker on an Ace-high flop. As the pre-flop aggressor, I will place a continuation bet of 400 (slightly over half the pot) to represent the Ace and put pressure on the Button. This allows me to take down the pot now if he missed or gather information about the strength of his hand based on his reaction.

I flopped top pair with a weak kicker (A4) on Ac7dJh. Flash c-bet 400 into 750, a reasonable half-pot sizing. My hand is too strong to fold - I have top pair. But raising is risky since my kicker is weak and I'd only get action from better hands (AJ, AK, AQ, AT). Calling in position to control pot size and see what develops is the best line.

I have middle pair (Jacks) with a King kicker on an Ace-high board. After my flop continuation bet was called, checking the turn is the most prudent play to control the pot size and realize my showdown value. The 3d is mostly a brick, but Claude's range likely includes weaker Aces or pocket pairs that won't fold to a second barrel. By checking, I can potentially induce bluffs from missed draws or get to the river more cheaply.

Flash c-bet flop then checked turn - this looks like a medium-strength hand or giving up. My A4 gives me top pair but weak kicker. I should check back for pot control. Betting risks getting check-raised by AJ/AK/AQ, and I don't need protection on this dry board. I'll see a free river and reevaluate.

I have middle pair (Jacks) on an Ace-high board. Claude called my pre-flop raise and flop continuation bet, but checked back on the turn when I checked to him. This indicates he likely has a hand with showdown value that isn't strong enough to bet for value, such as a weaker Jack, a middle pocket pair, or perhaps a very weak Ace. Since betting now would likely only get called by hands that beat me (like an Ace) and fold out hands I already beat, I will check to see a cheap showdown and realize my hand's value.

Flash raised preflop, c-bet flop, then checked both turn and river. This passive line suggests weakness - likely a medium-strength hand like Jx, a pocket pair, or a missed draw. My A4 has showdown value with top pair, but the weak kicker makes value betting risky. Betting could get called by better aces (AK/AQ/AJ) or induce a check-raise. The safest play is to check back and show down my top pair.

UTG position with 6s3d, a trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. Early position requires a tight range; folding is standard to avoid playing weak hands out of position postflop. No steals or bluffs justified here with 100BB stacks and multiple players behind.

I am in middle position with Queen-Four offsuit (Q4o). This is a very weak starting hand that does not belong in a standard opening range from this position. With a healthy stack of 13,300 chips and being the chip leader, there is no reason to play such a marginal hand and risk unnecessary losses. Folding is the correct strategy to preserve my stack for more favorable opportunities.
72 suited is a very weak hand. Even in late position with two folds ahead, this hand has poor playability and limited potential. Not worth investing 100 chips. Easy fold.

Folded to us on the button with K8s (Kh8h). This is comfortably within a BTN opening range, especially 6-handed, and we can profitably attack the blinds with position and decent playability (suited, reasonable high-card). I’ll use a standard steal sizing that pressures the blinds without risking too much if we face a 3-bet.

I am in the Small Blind with Qs3s facing a 2.5x raise from the Button. While Q3s is suited, it is a very weak hand with significant reverse implied odds due to the low kicker. Furthermore, playing out of position against an aggressive Button opener and potentially the Big Blind is difficult. The pot odds of 33% are not attractive enough to justify the positional disadvantage and hand weakness in the early stages of the tournament. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

I’m in the big blind with T♦4♥ facing a button open to 250. This is a weak, offsuit hand and not a 3-bet candidate, but defending the BB is cheap: calling costs 150 more (total 250 committed) and would make the pot 550, so I only need ~27% equity to break even. The button’s opening range is wide, and with deep-ish stacks I can realize implied odds and see a flop. Plan: call to see the flop, fold to heavy aggression if I miss, and proceed only with a ten, strong redraws, or good pot-control spots.

Board is J♠ J♥ 7♣ and I hold T♦4♥ — no pair, only a weak backdoor heart and negligible equity vs a button raiser’s range (which includes many Jx, overpairs, and sets). Pot is 550 and action is checked to me. This is a clear pot‑control spot: I don’t want to bloat the pot with air or bluff into likely callers out of position. I will check and fold to meaningful pressure. I might call a minuscule probe bet (very small sizing) for pot odds, but otherwise concede this street and wait for a cleaner spot to invest chips.

Board is J-J-J, so everyone has trips and the hand is mostly about kickers. With K8hh we have a relatively strong kicker for our BTN opening range (behind A-x and some Kx like KQ), and BB’s defend range contains a lot of low cards that are in bad shape versus a small c-bet. This is a great spot to use a small, range-ish continuation bet in position to pick up folds from the weakest kickers and get thin value from worse K/Q/high-card holdings, while keeping the pot controlled. If we face a meaningful check-raise, we can comfortably let it go because raises are heavily weighted to A-x / better kicker combos.

I checked the flop as planned and now face a 200 bet into the pot (pre-bet pot 550, pot now 750). Calling 200 would require roughly 200/(750+200) ≈ 21% equity to break even. With T♦4♥ on J♠ J♥ 7♣ I have essentially no showdown equity vs a button’s C-bet range (they’ll have many Jx, overpairs, 7x and decent turn cards) and I have no realistic backdoor draws. I’m out of position and don’t want to invest chips with air and face more pressure on later streets, so I fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

I am UTG with A2o. This is a very weak hand to open from early position in a 6-max game. It lacks playability, and even if I hit an Ace on the flop, I will frequently be out-kicked and dominated. Given my stack size and the disadvantageous position, the most disciplined and standard professional play is to fold.
68 offsuit in middle position is a weak hand. With 5 players behind me, this is an easy fold. Not worth risking chips with such marginal holdings.

We’re in the cutoff with 7♥3♦ (offsuit), a hand that’s well below a standard CO opening range. While we have positional advantage versus the blinds, the button still acts behind us and can punish a light steal with 3-bets, and if called we’ll frequently realize poor equity and get dominated. With deep stacks (~100bb), preserving chips and avoiding negative-EV marginal opens is higher priority than forcing a thin steal here. Best play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the Button with Queen-Four offsuit. The action has folded to me. While the Button is an advantageous position to steal the blinds, Q4o is simply too weak to open profitably. It has poor playability post-flop and is easily dominated. At this early stage of the tournament (Hand 6/150), there is no need to force plays with the bottom of my range. I will fold.

Heads-up from the small blind with 9♣4♦ — a weak hand but in heads-up play the range should be wide. Elon (BB) has shown a tendency to fold preflop in previous hands, so I have strong fold equity. A minimum raise to 200 is a low-commitment steal attempt that wins the 150-chip pot if he folds and still keeps my post-flop options flexible if he calls. If called I’ll proceed cautiously out of position (looking to c-bet favorable textures and fold to strong resistance). Given stacks (my 6,900) the small raise maximizes EV versus a fold-heavy opponent while limiting downside.

Preflop in BB facing a standard 2x open from SB after all others folded. AKo is a premium hand with excellent equity against SB's wide opening range (likely 40-50% of hands including Ax, broadway, suited connectors). 3-betting isolates the SB, builds the pot for value when ahead, and denies their equity with marginal hands. Calling OOP risks playing awkwardly postflop if we miss or face aggression. Raising to 600 (3x the open) is a balanced size, pot-committing neither player but pressuring a wide range. Folding is never correct.

Elon 3-bet to 600 after my 200 open; pot is 800 and I must call 400 (total commit 600). I hold 9♣4♦ — a weak, easily dominated hand and I’ll be out of position postflop. Although Elon has folded a lot preflop historically, this direct 3-bet is a clear sign of strength or at least a hand that plays much better against a caller than my rag. Calling for 400 gives decent pot odds but leaves me with poor playability and high reverse implied odds. 4-betting as a bluff (min to 1000) would risk a lot more chips with little equity if called. Preserve the stack and fold; wait for a better spot to exploit his fold tendency.

Pocket Aces UTG - the best starting hand in poker. I need to raise for value. Standard raise to 250-300 to build the pot while still getting callers. Going with 250 (2.5x BB) to look like a standard open and encourage action.

Claude is opening from UTG in a 6-max configuration, which is typically a relatively strong range. Q9o is dominated by his value region (AQ/KQ/QJ, better 9x), plays poorly multiway, and calling invites tough postflop spots from the blinds and later positions. With ~100bb stacks there’s no need to defend this marginal offsuit hand versus an UTG open; it’s not a good 3-bet candidate either due to weak playability when called. Preserve stack and wait for better spots.

I am holding Qh 2h in the Cutoff facing a raise to 250 (2.5bb) from the UTG player (Claude). Q2s is a very weak hand, particularly against an early position opening range which tends to be strong. Even though the hand is suited, the low kicker presents significant reverse implied odds problems if I hit top pair. Calling creates a risk of being squeezed by the players behind me or playing a dominated pot. The equity of Q2s against a standard UTG range is insufficient to justify a call. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

Claude opened UTG to 250, representing a fairly strong, tight range from early position. I hold 8♣4♦ offsuit — a weak, poorly coordinated hand with very little equity or postflop playability. Calling 250 would often leave me in multiway pots dominated, and a bluff-raise to 400 is too thin versus the raiser and the blinds. With a 6,750 stack I should conserve chips for better spots rather than invest here. Fold.

QdTs offsuit is a marginal hand at best from the small blind facing an UTG raise from Claude, who likely has a strong range. Out of position post-flop against a tough opener, and with the big blind yet to act (potential squeeze), the pot odds of calling 200 into 400 aren't sufficient given the poor playability and equity realization OOP. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities early in the tournament with deep stacks.

I am in the Big Blind with 9-6 offsuit. Claude has raised from Under-the-Gun (UTG) to 2.5 times the big blind. While I am getting decent pot odds (150 to call into a 400 pot), 9-6 offsuit is a very weak hand that performs poorly out of position, especially against an UTG opening range which is typically strong. I don't want to bleed chips defending with marginal hands early in the tournament. I will fold.

UTG 6-handed with Q5o is too weak and dominated by the continuing ranges (better queens, broadways, suited aces). It has poor playability and high reverse-implied-odds when we make a marginal top pair. Limping is undesirable and opening risks getting 3-bet or playing bloated pots out of position. Best EV is to conserve chips and fold.

I hold 8c 6s in Middle Position. This is a weak unsuited one-gapper with very low playability and negative expected value to open, especially with four players left to act behind me. There is no strategic merit in entering the pot with this hand. I will fold.

Cutoff with T♦9♦, 67.5BB effective (6750 chips). With 150 already in the pot and 100 to call, T9s is a very playable, positionally strong hand — good equity and implied odds. I want to apply pressure to the button and blinds, narrow the field, and take advantage of position postflop. A standard 3xBB open to 300 achieves that: it’s large enough to pick up folds from weak ranges but small enough to get action from worse hands I can outplay. Plan: if called, proceed with position and c-bet favorable flops; if I face a large 3-bet (significantly above a small 3-bet), I’ll fold and preserve my stack; consider calling small 3-bets depending on sizing and opponent. Raising here maximizes EV from this spot.

On the button with QhJh, a strong suited Broadway connector, facing a cutoff min-raise from Minni. Excellent position with deep stacks (~100BB). QJs has great playability post-flop, high equity vs Minni's wide cutoff opening range, and flush/straight potential. Calling realizes equity in position, keeps pot small, and allows post-flop flexibility. 3-betting is possible as a semi-bluff, but flatting is standard and balanced here early in the tournament.

I am in the Small Blind with J4 offsuit. Minni opened to 3bb from the cutoff and Elon called on the button. J4o is a very weak hand to play out of position against two opponents, even with the pot odds provided by the small blind discount. Folding is the most profitable long-term play here to preserve my stack.

Holding 4d8h in the BB facing a raise to 300 and a call. I need to call 200 more into a 750 pot (3.75:1 odds). However, 48o is a very weak hand that plays poorly multiway and will put me in difficult postflop situations. Even with the pot odds, this hand doesn't have good implied odds or playability. Folding is the disciplined play.

I raised preflop and the flop is 9h 6h Ac — I hold Td9d: middle pair (pair of nines) with a ten kicker. The board has two hearts and an Ace, so opponent’s calling range contains many overcards, heart draws, and Ax combinations. I want to both get value from worse hands (10x, weaker 9x, some hands with a heart draw) and deny free equity to heart draws/overcards. A standard medium c-bet (~half the pot) balances extracting value and controlling the pot size with a marginal made hand. I will bet 400 into the 750 pot. If I get called I’ll re-evaluate the turn (proceed cautiously if an Ace or a heart arrives). If I face a large raise I’ll fold most of the time to preserve my stack.

Flop gives me a strong queen-high flush draw (9 outs) plus live overcards on a draw-heavy board (two hearts). Pot is 1150, Minni c-bets 400 (~53% pot) as pre-flop raiser from cutoff, representing Ax, pocket pairs, or bluffs. In position with deep stacks (~60BB effective for her), raising as a semi-bluff is ideal: high equity (35%+ to improve), strong fold equity against her polarized range (weak Ax, draws, air fold often), builds pot when ahead, denies her equity. Calling is passive and allows her to realize equity cheaply. Folding is -EV with this playability. Raising to 1200 (3x her bet, ~75% pot) balances pressure and pot commitment early in tournament.

I opened from the cutoff with T♦9♦ and c-bet 400 on 9♥ 6♥ A♣. Elon check‑raised to 1,200 — a large sizing that heavily favors value (Ax, sets, 9x) with some two‑heart semi‑bluffs. To call 800 more I’m getting ~800/(2,350+800) ≈ 25.4% break‑even equity. My hand has roughly 5 clean outs (3 tens + 2 nines) → ~20% to improve to two‑pair/trips by the river, and several of those outs are not clean vs likely Ax (A‑T, A‑9) or sets. I’m out of position and will face more aggression on later streets. Raising to 2,000 would overcommit with a marginal hand and is unlikely to fold out many of his value hands. Calling is marginal and will put me in tough spots post‑turn. Preserve the stack and fold.

I am in UTG position with 7h Ts. This is a very weak off-suit hand (T7o) which is dominated by most ranges and has poor playability. In a 6-handed game, opening from UTG requires a much stronger range. Folding is the only standard and correct play.

7♣5♠ is a weak, offsuit, disconnected hand in middle position. Facing a 100 call into a 150 pot and with multiple players to act after me, this hand will be dominated or play poorly out of position; raising is spewy and calling to see a flop is negative EV. I’ll fold and wait for a better spot from late position or with a stronger holding.

Preflop in cutoff with 5h3c, a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low equity, and no straight or flush potential worth pursuing. UTG and MP folded, but limping or raising trash like this is -EV; blinds can defend wide. Deep stacks, early hand, standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

I am on the button with 6d4s, which is a very weak hand. All players before me have folded. While I could attempt a steal from the button, my hand has zero post-flop equity and the blinds (Claude and FiveTwo) have shown some willingness to play in previous hands. Given the early stage of the tournament (Hand 9 of 150) and my healthy stack, there is no need to take unnecessary risks with such a poor hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.
Everyone folded to me in the SB with Q9o. This is a good spot to raise and steal the blinds or play heads up against the BB. Q9o is well above the range I should be opening here. I'll raise to 250.

Claude is raising from the small blind in a folded-to spot, which is typically a wide steal range. Getting 150 to win a pot that will be 500 gives strong immediate odds, and Th2h has some playability via flush/straight potential even though it’s weak and often dominated when it makes one pair. No need to bloat the pot with a low-equity 3-bet; best is a defend-and-realize line and play fairly straightforward postflop (continue mainly on good equity boards).

I raised preflop and got called by the BB. Flop is K32 with two hearts. This is a good c-bet board - it's dry and king-high boards favor the preflop raiser's range. I have nothing but Q-high with backdoor hearts. A small c-bet around 1/3 pot should take this down often, and if called I can reevaluate on the turn.

Claude’s SB open is typically wide and this flop c-bet is small (175 into 500). With Th2h on Kh-3h-2d we have bottom pair plus a strong flush draw (9 hearts outs, plus 2 outs to trips), giving plenty of equity and good immediate pot odds (175 to win 850 after calling). Raising would bloat the pot OOP and mostly get action from Kx / better hands, so the best line is to call and realize equity, reassessing versus turn barrels and capitalizing on hearts.

Turn brought me second pair with the Qd. I now have queens with a 9 kicker. FiveTwo called my flop c-bet, suggesting they have something - possibly a King, a draw, or a weak pair. With second pair I have showdown value but betting into a potential King is risky. I'll check to control pot size and potentially induce a bluff or get to a cheap showdown.

Claude’s SB open is wide and his small flop c-bet followed by a turn check on K-high with a new Q strongly caps him toward a lot of give-ups / marginal one-pair hands that don’t want to build a big pot. With Th2h we have a strong flush draw plus a bit of showdown value, so betting here is a profitable semi-bluff: we can win the pot immediately versus his air, charge diamond draws and random floats, and when called we still have solid equity with 9 heart outs (plus some improvement outs like a deuce). I’ll use a controlled sizing that pressures his weak range without overexposing myself to a check-raise; if he does check-raise big, we can comfortably fold and preserve stack.

I turned second pair with Q9 on K32Q. FiveTwo called flop c-bet and now bets 450 into ~850 when I check. They could have a King, but this sizing feels like a probe bet exploiting my weakness. They could also have a heart draw, a weak pair, or be floating. With second pair I have too much showdown value to fold. Raising only gets called by better. Calling and evaluating river is the right play.

River brought me two pair - Queens and Nines! This is a significant improvement from second pair. The board is K-Q-9-3-2 rainbow. FiveTwo bet turn when I showed weakness, but now I have a strong hand. I should value bet - Kx hands might call thinking I'm bluffing, and any Qx with worse kicker pays off. Betting around 60% pot (~1000) extracts value from one-pair hands while not over-committing if they have a set or K-Q.

We arrive on the river with just a pair of deuces after our heart draw bricks. After we bet the turn and Claude check-calls, his range is heavily weighted toward at least one-pair hands (Kx/Qx), occasional slowplays, and some missed diamond floats. When he now leads 1000 into 1750, this sizing and line are much more consistent with value (Kx, KQ, Qx that feels safe vs missed hearts) than with a high-frequency bluff.

Calling 1000 to win 3750 requires ~27% equity; with bottom pair we’re basically only beating pure bluffs, and I don’t expect Claude to be bluffing at that rate given the turn check-call. Raising as a bluff is also poor since his Kx portion won’t fold. Best is to preserve stack and fold.

UTG with pocket 7s in a 6-handed game and ~60BB effective — this is a clear open-raise. I want to take the initiative, narrow the field, and get value from worse pairs and broadways while retaining fold equity. I’ll open to 3x the BB (300 total) — bigger than the minimum to discourage limp/calling-heavy multiway pots and to build a pot I can play postflop. Plan: if I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold or re-evaluate based on opponent/sizing; if called I’ll c-bet favorable textures and control size on scary boards.

Preflop, facing an UTG raise to 300 from Minni into a 450 pot. My hand is 8h5d offsuit, which is extremely weak with no high card value, connectivity, or suitedness. In middle position, against an early position raiser who has shown aggression in prior hands, this is an easy fold to preserve stack and avoid playing out of position with marginal equity. No bluffs or calls justified here.

I am in the Cutoff with 9d 6d. Minni opened to 300 from UTG. While suited connectors or gappers have some potential, 96s is too weak to call a 3x raise from early position, especially with several active players still to act behind me (Claude, FiveTwo, and Pro). Standard play in this position is to fold and wait for a better spot.
T2o is a very weak hand. Facing a UTG raise to 300, this is an easy fold even from the button. No reason to defend with such a weak holding against an early position raise.

Ah6h is too weak to flat out of position versus a UTG open, but it plays well as a light 3-bet: we block strong Ax (AA/AK/AQ), have suited equity when called, and Minni has shown willingness to open/fold under pressure. From the small blind we want a larger sizing to deny equity and avoid going multiway with the BB. If Minni 4-bet jams their ~57bb stack, we can comfortably fold.

I am in the Big Blind with T4s, facing a raise from UTG and a 3-bet from the Small Blind. This action indicates strong ranges from my opponents. My hand is far too weak to cold call a 3-bet, especially with the original raiser still left to act behind me who could 4-bet. The pot odds do not justify entering a large pot with a dominated hand out of position. Preserving my stack is the correct play here.

I opened UTG to 300 with pocket 7s and FiveTwo (SB) 3-bet to 1,100. Effective stacks are ~5,750 — this is a standard-sized 3-bet but not huge. 77 is a hand I prefer to call here rather than folding or committing more with a marginal holding: it plays well heads-up postflop, has set-mining value, and calling keeps weaker parts of the opponent’s 3-bet range (broadways, suited connectors, bluffs) in the pot. The pot is ~1,500 and calling 800 more for a total of 1,100 gives reasonable pot odds (~35%) and preserves stack flexibility. Plan: call to see a flop; if I hit a 7 or a very favourable, low, uncoordinated board I’ll bet for value/isolation; if I miss and face heavy continued aggression on high/connected boards I’ll proceed cautiously and fold when the action indicates I’m behind. I will only 4-bet or call a 5-bet shove if sizing/reads justify it — otherwise fold to extreme all-in pressure.

As the preflop 3-bettor, this flop still favors my overall range (overpairs, strong Ax, some big cards) even though UTG’s flatting range contains more 77–99 and 9x/7x than mine. With Ah6h I have only ace-high plus backdoor hearts, so I’m not looking to check and realize—I'd rather apply pressure to their unpaired broadways and some pocket pairs. A small c-bet keeps my range wide, risks little, and sets up profitable barrels on A/high turns or hearts while allowing an easy fold versus a check-raise.

I flopped a set (7♣7♦) on 9♥-7♠-2♦ after calling FiveTwo's SB 3-bet. His small flop stab (650 into ~2,950) looks like a wide c-bet that includes many worse hands (9x, KQ/AQ-type floats, medium pairs and some bluffs). A set is far ahead of most of that range, so I want to build the pot and charge worse hands while still leaving room to call a large shove. I will raise for value and protection — sizing to 2,000 chips (~3x his bet) should pull calls from top pairs and broadway hands but not commit my entire stack to a small check-raise. Plan: if he calls I keep extracting on safe turn runouts; if he jams or puts in a very large raise I’ll strongly consider calling with this set (only truly behind to 99 or a flopped full house).

Minni’s raise to 2000 in a 3-bet pot leaves them only 950 behind, which effectively commits their stack. That means we have very low fold equity if we jam, and calling is also poor because we’ll be forced to continue versus a turn shove with almost no equity. With Ah6h on 9h7s2d we have only ace-high with a thin backdoor flush and no meaningful draws, so this is a clear give-up spot and we preserve our stack for better opportunities.

UTG in 6-handed play with 79s suited connectors. Deep stacks (~100BB effective). This hand has good playability, straight and flush potential, and performs well multiway or heads-up postflop. Standard UTG opening range includes suited connectors like 78s+, and 79s is close enough with solid implied odds. Raising for initiative and fold equity rather than limping or folding, sizing to 2.5x BB to build pot controllably while denying equity to weaker hands.

UTG has opened with a 2.5x raise. A4o is a weak hand in this context, as it is likely dominated by the opener's range and is difficult to play profitably against an UTG raise with four players yet to act. Folding is the most prudent and standard play to preserve chips for better opportunities.
A6o in the cutoff facing a min-raise from UTG. Marginal hand but I have position and it's only 250 to call. A6o is borderline against UTG but the price is good. I'll call and see a flop in position.

Pocket kings on the button vs an UTG open and a cutoff flat is a clear value squeeze. Calling invites a multiway pot where our overpair can get outflopped and where it’s harder to realize value cleanly. A 3-bet also isolates the opener, charges the caller, and sets up an easy stack-off line versus most 4-bets given effective stacks ~70–100bb.

Sizing: with a caller in between, I want a larger in-position squeeze than a standard heads-up 3-bet—around 4.5–5x the open to deny good odds and reduce multiway frequency. 1200 accomplishes that while keeping worse hands (QQ-JJ, AK, some AQ) in and building the pot.

I hold AKo in the Small Blind, a premium hand. The action involves an UTG open, a CO call, and a large squeeze to 1200 by FiveTwo on the Button. FiveTwo has demonstrated aggressive squeezing tendencies in previous hands (Hand #3, Hand #10) but has also folded to pressure. My position is poor (SB), and just calling would invite a multi-way pot out of position, which is not ideal for AKo. FiveTwo's stack is 6125 (approx 61 BBs), while I have 9600. A cold 4-bet is the standard play here to isolate the squeezer, deny equity to the original raiser and caller, and capitalize on the dead money. Given the stack sizes and the 12 BB raise, a cold 4-bet jam (all-in) puts maximum pressure on the opponents and denies them the chance to realize equity with marginal hands. It also prevents me from playing a tricky post-flop spot OOP. I am ahead of the squeezer's range and flipping or blocking mostly anything else.

Facing a huge 4-bet/all-in to 9,600 after Elon opened and the button 3-bet, I’m in the BB with 2♣8♥ — one of the worst possible hands preflop. To call I must commit my entire stack (7,800). My hand has almost no playability postflop and very little equity vs a polarized 4‑bet/shove range. Pot odds require roughly ~29% equity to break even, which 2‑8 offsuit cannot provide against this action. Preserving my tournament life and waiting for a much stronger spot is the correct play. Fold.

Facing a massive 4-bet shove from SB Pro (nearly all-in for 9600 into ~5k pot pre-my decision) after my UTG open-raise to 250 and Button's 3-bet to 1200. 7c9c suited connectors have good playability in multiway pots with deep stacks, but here equity is crushed vs. Pro's likely premium range (AA/JJ+, AK/AQ). Pot odds to call 9350 into 11400 (~1.2:1) are poor, no meaningful implied odds with Pro all-in and others (FiveTwo 6125 behind, Claude deep but pot bloated). Shoving over (min 10950 > my 10700) changes nothing vs. strong range. History shows Pro selective but capable of aggression (Hand 1 UTG raise). Preserve 10k stack for superior spots in tournament; folding speculative hand facing action.

A6o facing a 4-bet all-in from the SB after a UTG raise and button 3-bet. Pro's 4-bet jam range here is extremely strong - likely AA, KK, QQ, JJ, AK. My A6o is completely crushed against this range. Even if I'm up against AK, I'm dominated. This is a trivial fold - no reason to risk my entire tournament life with such a weak holding.

We’re facing Pro’s 4-bet to 9600, but with only 6125 behind our decision is effectively all-in or fold. With KK this is an automatic stack-off: Pro’s 4-bet range is heavily weighted to QQ+/AK, and we’re far ahead of everything except AA. Even in the worst case (vs AA) we still have ~18% equity, and the dead money from Elon/Claude plus blinds makes folding a massive EV mistake. Since we can’t raise (stack shorter than the bet), we call off.

I am UTG with Jack-Trey offsuit, which is a very weak starting hand. Given my position and the fact that there are five players still to act behind me, this is an easy fold. I should preserve my stack for hands with much better equity and post-flop playability.

Q8o in middle position is a weak holding. With several players yet to act including a short-stacked Pro who might shove, and strong stacks in cutoff and blinds, this hand doesn't have enough equity to open profitably. Standard fold.

Unopened pot in the cutoff, but T5o is too weak and dominated to profitably open at a 6-max table. Even if we steal some blinds, we’ll often get defended by the blinds and be forced into low-equity, reverse-implied-odds spots. With a healthy stack and no need to force marginal steals, folding preserves EV and avoids unnecessary variance.

I am on the Button with a stack of 23.25 BBs (2325 chips). The action has folded to me. My hand is Jack-Four offsuit (Jh 4c), which is a very weak hand with poor playability. Given my relatively short stack compared to the opponents in the blinds (Minni with 7750 and Elon with 10600), and their demonstrated willingness to play pots aggressively in previous hands, attempting to steal the blinds here is too risky. There is no strategic merit in entering the pot with this holding. The standard and optimal play is to fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I have Kd-2h in the small blind and action is folded to me heads-up against the big blind (Elon). K2o is marginal but I prefer to seize the initiative rather than limp and play out of position. I will make the standard minimum preflop raise to 200 total to apply pressure and collect the blinds or play the flop with initiative. If Elon 3-bets large I will fold this hand; if he calls I will continuation-bet on favorable, uncoordinated flops and play cautiously on ace/high or highly connected boards.

Preflop in BB facing a min-raise to 200 from SB after all others folded. SB range is very wide for a steal attempt: any ace, broadway, pairs, suited connectors, etc. A4o blocks aces, has decent high card strength, and realizes equity well heads-up OOP. Pot odds are excellent: 100 to call into 300 (pot will be 400, 3:1 direct odds requiring ~25% equity, which we have easily vs. wide range). Folding is too tight in BB defense spot early in tournament. No need to raise with marginal hand. Call and see flop.

Flop Qs Kh 9c — I have K♦2♥: top pair with a very weak kicker. I'm heads-up vs the BB who flat-called my SB open, so his range is wide (broadways, Kx/Qx, medium pairs, floats). This is an uncoordinated, single-high board and a good spot to take the initiative with a c-bet to get value from worse Qx/9x and medium pairs and to charge any floats. I want meaningful fold equity but don't need to build the pot too large with a marginal kicker, so I will bet about half the pot (200 into 400). If he folds I take it down; if he calls I will re-evaluate on the turn and be prepared to fold to a large check-raise since my kicker is poor.

Preflop call was correct vs. wide SB min-raise range after folds behind. Flop Qs Kh 9c is somewhat coordinated (possible straights like JT, broadway draws), but SB range is very wide including Ax, broadway, pairs, suited connectors that miss. Ah4s is Ace-high with backdoor heart flush draw and some straight potential. Facing a small 200 bet into 600 (1/3 pot), pot odds are excellent: 200 to call into 800 (4:1, ~20% equity needed). We have ~25-35% equity vs. her polarized c-bet range (value: KQ, KT, Q9, sets; bluffs: missed suited connectors, weak Ax). OOP, but early tournament, cheap call to realize equity and control pot. Will fold to turn aggression if no improvement. Raising too thin without equity.

I have K♦2♥ on Q♠ K♥ 9♣ 6♣ — top pair but an extremely weak kicker. Elon flat‑called my preflop open and my flop c‑bet, so his range is wide (Kx with better kickers, Qx, 9x, J10 straights, medium pairs, and some two‑club hands that now have a one‑card flush draw). The turn club actually makes some draws possible and doesn’t improve my hand. He checked, showing weakness/pot‑control, and I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal kicker or face a check‑raise I’ll have to fold. I will check the turn to control the pot and keep my river options open: call reasonable river bets for thin value, fold to large aggression, or make a small value bet on safe rivers if he checks back.

Minni min-raised preflop from SB after folds, wide stealing range including Ax, broadway, pairs, suited connectors. Flop Qs Kh 9c hit some of her value (KQ, KT, Q9, sets) and draws (JT, T8s), she small c-bet 200 into ~600 for thin value/protection or probe. I called with Ah4s ace-high + backdoor equity, good vs polarized range. Turn 6c adds club flush draw, possible runner-runner straights (e.g., TJ), but no help to me. She checks turn, indicating possible weakness (missed draws, medium pairs like 88-TT/JJ without improving, weak Ax), trapping sets/top pairs, or giving up. Pot 800, deep stacks (my 10.3k, her 7.4k). With ace-high, decent showdown value vs her check-back range (bluffs/misses), no strong draw or made hand. Betting risks folding out worse (e.g., Qx, Jx, missed draws) while vulnerable to raises from better (Kx, sets, straights). Raising unnecessary OOP. Best: check behind to realize equity cheaply, pot control, see river showdown where ace-high often good early tournament. Avoid bloating pot with marginal hand.

River paired my deuce and I now have two pair (Kings and Deuces) with Kd2h on Qs Kh 9c 6c 2s. Elon flat‑called pre and the flop c‑bet then checked the turn, so his range is wide (single Kx, Qx, 9x, medium pairs, and missed draws). Two pair is well ahead of most of that range, so I want to extract value without bloating the pot against the relatively small number of hands that beat me (KQ/K9 or sets like 22/99/QQ). A half‑pot value bet (~400 into 800) is the right size — it will get called by worse one‑pair hands and thin value hands while keeping the price reasonable if he raises. Plan: bet 400; fold to a large, clear raise, call a single‑street cheap call.

Facing a half-pot river bet of 400 into 800 (pot odds ~3:1, needing ~25% equity) from Minni (SB) after she min-raised preflop (wide stealing range: Ax, Kx, broadway, pairs, suited connectors), small c-bet flop QsKh9c (hits some value/draws like KQ/KT/Q9/JT straight, but misses many), checked turn 6c (possible medium strength, missed draws, or trap), now leads river 2s (brings possible flush, but pairs board minimally). My Ah4s is ace-high with decent blocker (blocks AA/AK/AQ), showdown value vs her bluff-heavy range (missed clubs/spades, weak Ax, Qx/Jx/Tx pairs that don't improve). Board straights (e.g., JT) or sets beat me, but she checked turn suggesting not monster. Deep stacks (103bb), early tournament (hand 12/150), small pot (12bb); calling realizes equity cheaply vs polarized range (~30-40% equity est.). Folding too tight OOP vs small bet; raising thin/no fold equity. Call to pot control and showdown.
5c2c is a very weak hand, even suited. From UTG position, I need to play a tight range. This is an easy fold.

6-handed from middle position with 8♦4♣ is too weak and dominated to open, and limping creates multiway pots where we’ll realize equity poorly and face pressure from the button/blinds. With no dead money beyond blinds, best EV is to discard and wait for better spots.

At 23BB stack depth without antes, the pot odds for stealing are lower compared to an ante game. My hand, 6s7s (suited connector), is a reasonable opening hand from the Cutoff in deeper stacked games or with antes, but at this stack depth, it faces significant reverse implied odds and risk of re-shoves from the three large stacks behind me (Minni, Elon, Flash). Preserving my stack for high-card hands or better shove spots is the optimal strategy here. Opening light into aggressive big stacks is too risky.

I’m on the button with 7♥6♣ — a marginal but playable connector in position. Three players folded to me, so I can attempt a steal against the blinds. Both SB (Elon) and BB (Flash) have shown willingness to defend/3-bet at times, but my stack (86 BB) is deep enough to play postflop if called. I’ll open to a standard 3x (300) to apply pressure and get fold equity while keeping the pot controllable. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet, call small resistance rarely, and if called proceed to c-bet favorable flops (and play cautiously on Ace-high or highly coordinated boards).

Preflop in SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB) with A5o. A5 offsuit is a marginal hand with poor post-flop playability, dominated by better aces, and unlikely to make strong hands. Pot odds are decent at ~1.8:1 effective, but BB still to act and may squeeze or call wide. Defending SB wide is possible against button opens, but A5o is a standard fold here to preserve stack early in tournament with deep stacks (~100BB). No reason to raise or call; folding is optimal to wait for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack-Eight offsuit. Minni, who has been quite active throughout the tournament, raised to 300 from the Button. This is a 3x open. While I am getting decent pot odds (200 to call into a pot of 450), J8o is a weak hand to play out of position against an aggressive opponent. To avoid putting myself in difficult post-flop situations with a marginal hand, I will choose to fold and wait for a better spot.

We’re first to act in a 6-max configuration with a marginal suited hand (T4s). From UTG our opening range should stay relatively tight; T4s performs poorly when called/3-bet, is dominated by better Tx/4x suited combos, and doesn’t realize equity well out of position across multiple streets. With no compelling ICM/stack-pressure spot forcing action, the best EV play is to fold and preserve position and stack for stronger opens.

I hold 4c 3d in middle position, which is an extremely weak hand (low unsuited connector). My stack is currently 23.25 BBs. There is no strategic justification for opening this pot or limping, as the hand has very little playability and I am out of position against the button and cut-off. I will fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

7♠4♦ is a very weak, non-playable hand from the cutoff. Although opening here can be profitable against passive blinds, the Button (Elon) and SB (Flash) are capable defenders/3-bettors and Claude in the BB defends often — I don't want to risk a multiway or a 3-bet with essentially no equity. Calling 100 to see a flop is also poor value given the hand's playability. With ~87.5 BB effective and the tournament still early, preserve chips and wait for a better spot.

Preflop on the button in an unopened pot. My hand is 6h3s, which is offsuit, unconnected, and low cards with very poor playability postflop. Even though button stealing is profitable with a wide range against blinds, 63o is below the standard min-raise range (typically 40-50% hands like pairs, suited aces, broadways, strong suited connectors, and some offsuit connectors starting from 76o or so). Equity realization is low if called, and postflop dynamics are tough in position. Opponents like Claude have shown passive calling tendencies in history, increasing risk. Better to fold and wait for stronger spots to build stack, avoiding unnecessary variance early in tournament.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Ten of clubs, a very strong hand for a blind-vs-blind battle. Everyone has folded to me. I will raise to 300 to exert pressure on the Big Blind and build a pot with a hand that has great playability and high equity in this head-up scenario.

62s is a very weak hand. Even getting 3:1 odds to call 200, this hand has poor playability and will often make weak pairs or weak draws. Flash raised from SB which could be a wide range, but 62s is too weak to defend profitably. Folding to avoid losing chips with a bottom-tier hand.

I am holding Q8o in UTG position with a stack of roughly 23 big blinds. This hand is significantly below the standard opening range for early position in 6-max hold'em, as it is easily dominated by hands that call or raise behind me (KQ, AQ, 88+, etc.). Playing this hand out of position with a vulnerable stack size is -EV. The correct play is to fold and preserve chips for a better opportunity.

A♣7♠ is a playable ace in middle position — better to take the initiative than limp or fold. UTG has folded, I have ~87bb so I can open-raise and play postflop. I’ll raise to 3x the BB (300 total) to get fold equity vs the blinds, keep the pot manageable if I’m called, and discourage random limp-calls that reduce my equity. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold this hand; if I get called I’ll c-bet on favorable A-high or dry flops and pot-control on coordinated boards.

JT suited in cutoff is a strong speculative hand with good playability post-flop, including straight and flush potential. Facing a middle position raise to 300 into a 450 pot offers good implied odds with deep stacks. Calling keeps the pot multi-way potentially, preserves position on button and blinds, and avoids bloating the pot OOP if 3-bet. Folding is too tight early in tournament; 3-betting risks isolation against Minni's likely decent range without premium blockers. Standard call to realize equity.

I am holding 2c 9d on the button. This is a very weak hand. Minni opened to 3 big blinds from middle position and Elon called. With a weak hand and action ahead of me, there is no strategic justification for calling or raising. Folding is the only sensible move to preserve my stack.

KQo is a strong hand. Facing a raise from Minni and a call from Elon, I'm getting good odds to call (250 into 750). Being OOP in the SB is a disadvantage, but KQo plays well enough here. Could 3-bet to ~1000-1100, but calling keeps the pot manageable while still having a premium hand. I'll call and see a flop.

We’re getting a good immediate price (200 to win 1000), but 34o is near the bottom of our defend range and plays terribly multiway and out of position versus an opener plus two callers. Realizing equity will be very difficult, and when we make a pair it’s often dominated with high reverse implied odds. No profitable squeeze option either given the multiway dynamic and hand quality. Preserve stack and wait for better spots.

Flopped second pair (Kings) with Q kicker on Kc Ah 3c. The Ace is concerning as Minni opened and Elon called - both could easily have an ace. I'm OOP in a 3-way pot with a vulnerable made hand. Two clubs on board means flush draws are possible. Checking to see how action develops is prudent - if they check behind, I can reevaluate on turn. If bet, I can decide whether to continue with second pair.

I opened preflop and flop paired my Ace: A♣7♠ on K♣ A♥ 3♣ gives me top pair (with a medium/weak kicker) and a backdoor club possibility. I’m the preflop raiser with two callers (SB Claude and Elon behind me), so I should take the initiative and c-bet for value and to charge club draws. Because it’s multiway I don’t want to overcommit — a half-pot bet extracts value from worse A-x and K-x hands, charges draws, and keeps the pot manageable if I face aggression. If I get called I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (pot-control vs coordinated runouts); if I face a large raise I’ll re-evaluate and likely fold to extreme aggression without improvement.

JT suited clubs flops a strong flush draw (9 clean outs to the nut flush if no K/3, but solid anyway) on a Kc Ah 3c board, which is draw-heavy with possible A-high or K-high hands from Minni's MP raise range. Pot odds are excellent at 1500:500 (3:1, needing ~25% equity; flush draw alone ~35% vs random range, more vs polarized). Positionally advantaged in CO with Button out. Calling keeps pot controlled, induces from weaker draws/A-high, allows Claude's action behind (loose cold-caller preflop), and maximizes implied odds from deep stacks (~80-100BB). Raising risks isolation OOP on draws or vs monsters; folding is spew given equity/odds. Multiway potential cheapens effective price. Plan: call, check turn if miss unless good price, reassess river.

I have second pair (Kings) with Q kicker on Kc Ah 3c. Minni bet 500, Elon called. The Ace is concerning - both could have an Ace. However, I'm getting 5:1 pot odds (500 to win 2000). Elon's flat call suggests he may have a draw or medium hand rather than a strong Ace. Raising would only get called/raised by better hands. Folding is too weak given pot odds. Calling is the correct play to see the turn and re-evaluate.

The turn Kh is huge for me - I now have trip Kings with Queen kicker. This is a monster hand. I'm first to act with 2500 in the pot. I should bet for value. Minni likely has Ax and Elon could have draws or weak aces. Leading out lets me build the pot and extract value from worse hands. Betting around 60-65% pot (~1600) balances value and keeping them in.

I have A♣7♠ on Kc Ah 3c Kh — that’s two pair (A+K) with a 7 kicker. Claude check‑called the flop and now spikes the turn to 1600. His line is consistent with very strong hands (Kx turned trips/quads or AK turning a full house, occasionally slowplayed pocket pairs), but combos that actually beat me are limited. The pot is 4100 and calling 1600 gives ≈28% break‑even equity — well within reach for my two‑pair vs his likely raising range. Raising here would bloat a multiway pot, polarize my range, and risk getting 3‑bet or folding out the weaker hands I want value from. I’ll call 1600 to control the pot, keep worse A/K hands and draws in, and re‑evaluate on the river (fold to extreme aggression unless the runout improves me or the read changes).

Nut club flush draw on paired kings turn board in a multiway pot. Pot odds are excellent (5700:1600 ~3.5:1 effective, needing ~22% equity; 9 clean outs for ~19% by river, plus fold equity from position if raise later). Claude's SB donk turn after checking flop suggests possible weak Kx, turned pair (like A3/K3 unlikely), or trapping monsters (AK/KQ), but range polarized with bluffs/missed draws. Minni's flat pre/TF/call flop/call turn caps at likely top pair (AK/AQ) or better, but calling keeps pot controlled OOP to them while retaining position. Raising risks isolation vs monsters or free cards for their equity; folding spews given live draws and implied odds from their ~8k stacks. Plan: call, evaluate river board texture; if club, bomb; if not, check-fold vs aggression or small value bet thin.

I have trip Kings with Queen kicker - a very strong hand. Both Minni and Elon called my 1600 turn bet, suggesting they likely have Ax hands or possibly draws. The 6h river completes a potential heart flush (Ah-Kh-6h on board). My hand is too strong to check - I need to extract value from worse hands like Ax. However, with two callers and a possible flush, I should bet for thin value. Betting around 45% pot (~3300) targets Ax hands while not overcommitting if someone backed into a flush or has a boat.

Board Kc Ah 3c Kh 6h — I hold A♣7♠: two pair (A+K) with a mediocre kicker. Claude’s line (check-call flop, turn raise to 1600, now a river bet/raise to 3300) is exactly the line for Kx that turned trips and for very strong hands (AK, KK, AA/full houses/quads). The pot is 10,600 and calling 3,300 gives only ~24% break‑even equity, but against Claude’s polarized/value-heavy range my actual showdown equity is very poor — I mainly beat rare bluffs or worse A‑x kickers. Raising to commit my stack would be reckless (he calls with all the hands that beat me; fold equity is small). Fold and preserve chips for a clearer spot.

Facing Claude's river bet of 3300 into 7300 (pot now 10600), with JcTc for pure jack-high air after missing the nut club flush draw on a paired kings board with completed heart flush. Preflop and flop suited connectors justified speculative call for playability and implied odds multiway deepstacked, turn call followed plan given pot odds and position despite OOP action, but river aggression from Claude (SB donk turn after check/call flop, now betting after Minni folds) repolarizes to strong value: likely trips (Kx like KQ/KJ/AK), two-pair (A3/K3 unlikely), or nut heart flush (QhJh/10h9h/etc.). No showdown value, negligible equity (~0% vs value range, <10% vs bluffs), pot odds 4.2:1 require ~19% which isn't met. Raising has no fold equity vs committed villain pot-committing OOP with 1675 behind, risks stack-off vs monsters. Standard check-fold plan executed: fold preserves 7450 stack (~74BB) early in tournament (hand 15/150), avoids spew, lives for better spots. Opponent tendencies: Claude loose preflop (cold-calls OOP), capable of donk value.

UTG in a 6-max spot with A♣? Actually A♥4♦ (A4o). With ~63bb effective this small ace is marginal but playable — it has blocker value and reasonable showdown equity, and I want initiative rather than limp. I’ll open to 300 (≈3x) instead of just calling 100: this size gets fold equity, narrows the field, and avoids uncomfortable multiway pots out of position. Pro in the BB is short (~22bb) and may shove; I have decent equity vs a short-stack shove and will call such an all-in. Versus a sizable 3-bet from a big stack I’ll fold and re-evaluate; postflop I’ll c-bet favorable dry boards and play cautiously on wet/connected textures.

Pocket tens is a strong premium hand facing an UTG raise from Minni. Standard play is to 3-bet for value and to isolate, thinning the field and building the pot. Calling risks a multiway pot out of position, which is suboptimal with TT. A 3-bet to 900 (3x the open) is appropriately sized given the pot and stacks; Minni has 6050 and can call lighter ranges, while we can fold to a 4-bet or play postflop if called. This maximizes equity realization and fold equity early in the tournament.

I am holding Jack-Three offsuit in the Cutoff. Minni opened for 3x from UTG and Elon 3-bet to 900. This hand is significantly outside of any reasonable calling or 4-betting range in this position against two early/middle position players showing strength. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

A9o on the button facing a 3-bet to 900. Minni opened UTG and Elon 3-bet. A9o is a marginal hand here - not strong enough to 4-bet but has decent playability in position. Getting roughly 1.5:1 on a call with position makes flatting reasonable. I have a comfortable stack and can play postflop well.

We’re in the small blind facing an UTG open, a 3-bet from Elon, and a cold call from Claude. 7-2 offsuit has extremely poor playability and equity multiway, and we’ll be out of position against a strong range in an inflated pot. Calling 850 to see a flop is a clear -EV leak, and raising as a squeeze with this hand is unjustified given the strength shown and the likelihood of continuing action. Preserve stack for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with [2s 6d], a very weak off-suit hand. The action is heavy: UTG raised to 300, Middle Position 3-bet to 900, and the Button cold-called 900. Although the pot odds are roughly 3.8:1, my hand has extremely low equity against three opponents who have shown significant strength, particularly the 3-bettor. I am also out of position against everyone except the UTG opener (post-flop order is Me, UTG, MP, BTN). Committing nearly 40% of my remaining stack (800 chips out of 2225) with 2-6 offsuit in a 3-bet multi-way pot is not a profitable play. I must preserve my stack for a better spot.

I opened UTG to 300 with A♥4♦ and Elon 3‑bet to 900; Claude cold‑called 900. It’s back on me for 600 (pot ~2250) or I can 4‑bet to 1500. With ~60bb effective A4o is marginal and plays very poorly multiway and out of position. Calling 600 requires roughly ~21% equity to break even, but I’m often dominated by the 3‑bettor’s value range (AK/AQ/TT+) and I’ll have difficult post‑flop decisions OOP to two opponents. 4‑betting as a light bluff is risky (I have limited blockers and could face a 5‑bet shove or cold call and be crushed). My earlier plan was to fold to a sizable 3‑bet — I’ll preserve my stack for a better spot rather than getting involved in a tough multiway pot. Action: fold.

Preflop 3-bet with TT as planned, isolating vs UTG open, and got called by Claude on the button. Flop Ac 9h 7d is Ace-high with possible straight draws (T8, J8, JT) and backdoor flush draws, but relatively dry. TT is a strong overpair to the 9-7 but vulnerable to Ax, which is heavily in Claude's 3-bet calling range (AJ+, suited Aces, some bluffs). As preflop aggressor OOP, a continuation bet for thin value and protection is standard, charging draws and worse pairs/pockets while building the pot when ahead. Betting ~2/3 pot (1500) into 2250 balances our range (sets, two-pair, Ax, bluffs) and denies equity to floats/draws. Check risks giving free cards or facing a bet where we have tough decisions. Folding equity also present vs his floaty button range. Early in tournament, variance acceptable with strong hand.

I flopped two pair (Aces and Nines) on A-9-7 rainbow. This is a monster against Elon's 3-bet range which likely contains overpairs, AK, AQ. He's betting 1500 into the pot - I need to raise for value. If he has AA/99/77 I'm beat but that's unlikely. Most of his range is AK/AQ/JJ/QQ/KK which I'm crushing. Raising to around 4000 extracts value while keeping worse hands in.

Facing a flop 3-bet from Claude on an Ace-high board (Ac 9h 7d) after my continuation bet with TT. Preflop, TT is a strong 3-bet candidate vs UTG open, and c-bet is standard as an overpair OOP. However, Claude's button call of my 3-bet suggests a wide range including Ax, broadway, pockets, suited connectors. His flop raise to 4000 (into ~3600 pot) polarizes to strong value like Ax (AJ+, A9/A7 suited possible), sets (77/99/AA), two-pair combos, or bluffs (missed draws, air). TT is an overpair but crushed by top value Ax holdings (~80% of his value range). Pot odds are ~3:1 (2500 to call into 7750), requiring ~25% equity, but vs his range TT has roughly 20-30% at best, with poor implied odds OOP and committed stacks afterward (2550 left into 10k pot). No strong history of Claude bluffing here, board unfavorable, early tournament (hand 16/150, 50BB deep), better spots exist. Fold preserves stack for premium situations.

K5 suited is a speculative hand with poor playability out of position from UTG in a 6-handed game. It lacks strength preflop and would be difficult to play postflop against multiple opponents likely to enter the pot. Standard tight strategy dictates folding to preserve stack and avoid marginal situations early in the tournament. Pot odds (calling 100 into 150) are not compelling for this hand.

I am holding 10-4 offsuit in middle position. This is a very weak hand that does not play well post-flop and lacks the equity to justify an opening raise or a call. With several active players behind me, including large stacks like Claude and FiveTwo, folding is the standard and correct play.
