Minni's line (UTG raise, flat BB 3-bet pre, check-call flop c-bet, check turn) strongly reps Ax (pair aces or better), AK (two pair), or premium pairs QQ-JJ (which I beat). Any Ax beats my hand, AK crushes, QQ-JJ I beat. Her range is face-heavy/tight given pre-flop action, and check-call flop denies many bluffs/draws. Pot 7975 with Pro's ~1375 dead money (wide shove range we both crush, ~80%+ equity vs him). Minni has 1875 left (~1/4 pot), I'm in position. Betting risks getting called/shoved by better (low fold equity on her capped strong range), bloating pot OOP effectively. Checking realizes equity vs Pro's side pot, controls pot, avoids raising her strong range, and gets a free river card. If river bricks/no scary card, can value bet thin or check showdown; villainous check might induce bluffs. Long-term EV+ to check marginal top pair on coordinated turn when checked to by strong range.

I have A♦Q♦ — top pair with a good kicker on A♥ 3♦ K♠ 7♦ 8♣. Only Pro (all‑in) and Elon are in the hand; Pro is already committed so my decision is effectively heads‑up vs Elon. Elon bet the flop large then checked the turn — line consistent with Kx, a medium Ax, or a turned-down bluff; many of his plausible hands (Kx, worse Ax, medium pairs/pocket pairs) will call a relatively small shove on the river. I’m short‑stacked (1,875) into a 7,975 pot, so shoving for 1,875 is a thin‑value shove that extracts calls from worse hands and prevents giving free showdowns. The only clean hands that confidently beat me are AA/AK/two‑pair combos, which are either already committed or less likely; if an opponent raises, they’re usually ahead anyway and I’m priced to risk losing. Decision: shove my stack to maximize value and fold equity — bet all‑in 1,875.

River board Ah-3d-Ks-7d-8c completes no strong draws (diamond flush missed, no completed straights like 5-6 or 9-T obvious, 8c somewhat blank). Holding top pair K9, vulnerable to Ax (beats us), two-pair combos (AK, A7 unlikely given preflop), or sets, but her line (UTG raise, flat short BB 3-bet pre which polarizes to strong Ax/premiums, check-call my flop c-bet on K-high board, check turn bringing flush draw, now all-in 1875 ~1/4 pot river) caps her at likely Ax (AT+, AQ+, AJs), KK, or occasional KQ/KJ which we beat, mixed with some bluffs like missed draws (QJ, JT for gutshot pre-turn), air from float, or J9/T9 for gutshot+backdoor. Preflop flat of BB 3-bet suggests cautious strength not premium monsters (less AA), and check-call flop denies many speculative hands but includes medium Kx. History shows Minni loose-aggressive (frequent raises/calls), capable of thin value/value-heavy lines (e.g., Hand 12 river bet called). Pot 9850 + her 1875 all-in + Pro's dead money (~80% equity vs Pro crushed); calling 1875 into 11725 (~6.25:1 odds, need 14% equity). Conservatively, even vs polarized range (60% value where we lose 70%, 40% bluffs we win 100%) equity ~38%>14%, massively +EV. Tournament mid-stack (5375 eff ~50BB pre-call), dead money juice, no reverse implied odds post-all-in, position for showdown value. Folding top pair too nitty OOP history, call realizes equity and builds stack variance positively over 150 hands.

I am UTG with Jh6h. In a 5-handed game, this hand is generally too weak to open, especially with several active players and the chip leader behind me. My hand lacks the connectivity and high-card strength required for a solid opening range from this position. Folding is the standard and most prudent play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

J4s is a weak hand, especially from UTG+1 position. While suited, the lack of connectivity and being in early position makes this a clear fold. Not worth risking chips with 4 players yet to act behind me.

Button unopened pot with two folds behind us; blinds are relatively tight/passive overall and BB (Elon) is the short stack, so we benefit from stealing and playing in position. T9o is a reasonable button open that can realize equity well when called by the SB, and using a small raise keeps risk controlled while still applying pressure to the blinds (and avoids bloating the pot vs a potential BB jam). Plan: open, then generally fold to a large 3-bet/jam from the BB given stack depth (~34bb).

Button opened to 250 (2.5x). In the small blind with A♠2♥ I'm OOP vs the button and have a weak kicker — a marginal 3-bet candidate at best. 3-betting OOP will enlarge the pot with a hand that's difficult to play postflop and may induce a shove or 4-bet from Elon (34BB), putting me in tough spots. Calling keeps the pot controllable, lets me realize equity and capitalize when I hit an Ace or backdoor draws, and allows me to fold to clear strength from opponents. I'll call the raise and re-evaluate after the big blind acts and depending on the flop texture.

Preflop in the big blind with Ac7s facing a button raise to 250 from FiveTwo and a small blind call from Minni. Pot is 600, I need to call 150 more (total 250 from me). A7o is a strong defensive hand here due to ace blocker, decent high card strength, and playability postflop. FiveTwo's button raise is likely wide (aggressive player history), Minni's call also loose from SB. Pot odds are excellent (~4:1), requiring only ~20% equity, which A7o crushes vs. typical button opening range (~40-50% hands) and holds up vs. calling range. Raising is unnecessary risk with 34bb stack; folding too tight OOP but with position postflop. Plan: Call, see flop in position, play aggressively if ace hits or control pot otherwise.

Board is 7h 7d 8h; I hold A♠2♥ in the small blind and am OOP. I have only Ace-high (one heart) and an essentially meaningless backdoor heart possibility — no made pair, no realistic draw. Leading out (a donk bet) would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and invite calls or shoves from hands that currently beat me (pairs, overpairs, sets) or commit the short stack. Checking keeps the pot under control, lets me see how the button and the short-stacked big blind respond, and preserves fold equity if I pick up equity on the turn. Plan: check and call small bets to realize my (very limited) showdown equity, fold to large raises/shoves where I have too few clean outs, and re-evaluate on the turn — if I pair my Ace or pick up a clearer draw I can play more aggressively then.

Monster flop with top set (trips 7s with Ace kicker) on a draw-heavy board (paired 7s, heart flush draw, possible 6-9 straight draws, 9x gutshots). FiveTwo's button raise preflop is wide given history of aggression, Minni's SB call also loose. Pot 750, my stack 3250 effective. Betting for thin value against overpairs/one-pair hands while charging draws heavily. Check-raising possible but betting builds pot OOP and denies equity. Size ~2/3 pot (500) balances value/protection without overbetting into potential monsters (unlikely but 88 FH, pocket 7s quads possible though tiny %). Plan to barrel turns/rivers if villain shows strength with worse.

We’re getting a very reasonable price: calling 500 to win a pot that will be ~2250 gives ~22% immediate pot odds. Even though T9o has no hearts, we still have a gutshot to the 6 plus two overcards that can pair and be best sometimes, and we have position to realize equity and potentially take it away on later streets when checked to. Raising is unattractive here because BB is short (likely to jam over a raise), and this paired/wet texture (7-7-8 with hearts) connects well with his leading range (7x/8x/heart draws), so a bluff-raise doesn’t generate enough folds and risks getting blown off our equity. Call and re-evaluate turn based on sizing/card and whether SB continues.

Call 500. I’m OOP with A♠2♥ on 7h‑7d‑8h: no made hand and only a one‑card heart (so only a runner‑runner flush) — my realistic way to win is pairing an A or 2 by the river (~24% chance). After Elon’s 500 and FiveTwo’s call the pot becomes ~2750, so calling 500 requires ≈15% equity to break even. My chance to improve exceeds that, so calling to realize equity is +EV. Raising OOP with a marginal hand would bloat the pot and could induce a shove from Elon’s short stack; folding surrenders too much equity. Plan: call the 500, re‑evaluate on the turn — continue/raise if I pair or pick up a clear draw, fold to large turn aggression if I don’t improve.

Check. On the turn (7h 7d 8h 3d) with A♠2♥ I have no made hand and only Aces or deuces (6 outs) to pair on the river — roughly 13% equity. There is no realistic flush or straight now, and I’m out of position against two players who both called the flop bet, so a bluff here is unlikely to fold them and would just bloat the pot. The pot is 2250; betting would commit me with very little equity. I’ll check to control the pot and see the river. If someone bets I’ll fold to large sizes and only call relatively small river/turn bets (roughly up to ~400 chips given my ~13% chance to improve); if I pair the river I can value-bet appropriately.

Top set with Ac kicker on a paired board, still the nuts against realistic ranges (quads or specific FH very unlikely). Flop bet got two callers: FiveTwo's button call fits wide range including overpairs (JJ+, AQ+), mid pairs, stubborn draws (hearts, 56/9T); Minni's SB call from loose history also fits worse hands/draws. Turn 3d is a low brick, completes no strong draws (diamond flush unlikely without preflop spade/heart tendencies), kills some gutshots but doesn't change much. Pot 2250, my stack 2750 effective vs both deep. Minni checks (weak, possibly trapping or giving up draws). Betting ~2/3 pot (1500) extracts value from overpairs/one-pair hands (88 set is possible but we have blocker to Aces and superior kicker if trips showdown), charges remaining flush draws, and builds pot for river while leaving room behind. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs but risks missing value from calling stations like Minni/FiveTwo history shows. Barrel plan continues as they showed strength by calling flop. Size balances value/protection without committing off.

Turn is still very coordinated and paired (7-7-8-3 with two hearts). After BB leads flop multiway and fires big again on the turn, his range is heavily weighted to strong made hands (7x, 8x/88) and strong draws that don’t fold. Against any 7x/8x we realistically have only the 4 outs to a 6 (pairing T/9 doesn’t beat trips or even 8x+7x two-pair), so our equity is far below the ~29% needed to call 1500 into 5250. With Minni still behind (who also called flop), our realized equity gets even worse and we can’t profitably bluff/raise into a range that will continue often. Best play is to preserve stack and fold.

I have A♠2♥ on 7h‑7d‑8h‑3d: no made hand and only 6 clean outs (any A or 2) to improve on the river → ~13% chance. Pot is 3750 and Elon bet 1500, so calling 1500 would contest a pot of 5250 — break‑even equity ≈ 1500/5250 ≈ 28.6%. My ~13% equity is far below the price, so a call is -EV. Raising as a bluff is unattractive: I’m out of position, Elon’s line (betting flop and turning up the pressure) looks value‑heavy, FiveTwo folded to the turn, and I’d be risking a large chunk of my stack with very little protection or fold equity. Followed my earlier plan to fold to large turn aggression when I didn’t improve. Fold and preserve chips for better spots.

K5o from UTG is a weak hand. Even though I have position on the blinds, opening K5 offsuit from under the gun is too loose - it will get dominated by better kings and I'll be out of position against later players who call or 3-bet. Standard fold.

5-handed and second to act, A5 suited is comfortably in my opening range. It has strong playability (nut-flush potential, wheel/straight draws) and benefits from fold equity preflop; limping just invites a multiway pot from the blinds and button. I’ll open-raise to a standard size to pressure the blinds and define ranges while keeping the pot manageable if I’m 3-bet.

Fold. 3♠4♦ is junk vs an UTG+1 open — very poor playability and often dominated. Calling 250 into a 400 pot gives break-even equity ~38%; against an early-position opener (FiveTwo) my actual equity is well below that and Elon (SB) or Flash (BB) can still squeeze or isolate, turning this into a marginal, multiway pot I don’t want to play. A tiny reraise to 400 won’t credibly fold better hands. With a healthy stack (≈110 BB) and plenty of hands left, preserve chips for a better spot on the button — fold preflop.

Preflop from the small blind facing an early position raise to 250 (200 to call) from FiveTwo, who has been aggressive in prior hands but from a strong position. My hand Qc6d is offsuit, uncoordinated, and has poor post-flop playability out of position. Pot odds are insufficient (400 pot, calling 200 for ~6.7:1 implied but low equity vs EP range). Folding preserves stack early in tournament (hand 36/150), avoiding speculative defense OOP with trash. No history suggests FiveTwo is overly wide to justify a wide defend.

I am in the big blind with 9s4h. FiveTwo raised to 250 from UTG+1. This is a very weak hand and does not play well out of position against a range that opened from early position. Even though I only have 150 more to call into a pot of 400, 9-4 offsuit is near the bottom of my range and is easily dominated. Folding is the most profitable long-term play here to preserve my stack.

5-handed UTG is relatively late-position; A4s is strong enough to open with good playability and nut-flush potential. The ace blocks a lot of 3-bet/value continues (AK/AQ/AA) which slightly improves our steal profitability. With stacks deep enough (we cover Elon), raising also lets us realize equity and take initiative versus blinds that can overfold. I’ll use a standard 2.5bb open to pressure the blinds without overinvesting.

I have 6♠2♣ in UTG+1 facing an UTG open to 250. This is an unplayable, easily dominated hand out of position against the raiser with three players left to act (BTN, SB, BB). Pot is 400 and a call of 250 requires ~250/(400+250) ≈ 38.5% equity — far more than 6‑2o has vs a UTG opening range, and multiway action only reduces my playability and implied odds. With a healthy 10,975 stack I should conserve chips for better spots. Fold.

ATo is a premium hand on the button facing an UTG raise from FiveTwo, who has shown aggression in previous hands but folds to resistance at times. Position is excellent, providing postflop advantage. 3-betting isolates the raiser, applies pressure with fold equity against a strong but polarized UTG range, and gets value from worse aces, broadways, and pairs. Sizing to 800 (3.2x the raise) is standard, building the pot while preserving stack depth (4950 chips, ~50BB). Calling is viable but 3-betting maximizes value and fold equity in a tournament spot early in the hand sequence.

I am in the Small Blind with a pocket pair of Deuces. While small pairs have value for 'set-mining,' the math does not support a call here. Elon (Button) has 3-bet to 800, and his total stack is only 4,150. To call, I must commit 750. For set-mining to be profitable, I generally need implied odds of at least 10:1 or 15:1. Here, Elon's remaining stack is only about 4.5 times the amount I need to call (3,350 / 750). Furthermore, FiveTwo (UTG), the original raiser, is still to act and could 4-bet, forcing me out of the pot. Being out of position for the rest of the hand against a strong range and a short stack makes this a clear fold.

J4o is a weak hand facing a 3-bet. Getting about 2.7:1 pot odds (700 to win 1900), but J4o has poor equity against a 3-betting range and doesn't play well post-flop. Elon has been aggressive and has a shorter stack (4150). Easy fold.

We opened A4s UTG, but Elon's 3-bet to 800 is quite large relative to stacks and effectively sets up a low SPR spot (if we call, SPR ~2). Out of position, A4s realizes equity poorly versus a button 3-bet range that is likely value-heavy (and his short stack encourages him to continue/jam rather than fold). A 4-bet bluff would need an unrealistically high fold frequency given his remaining 41bb stack and the sizing dynamics. Best is to cut losses and avoid reverse-implied-odds spots.

UTG with 4♠5♥ offsuit — a very weak hand to open or limp from first position. I’d be out of position against four opponents and the table has shown frequent preflop aggression, so calling 100 to play a multiway pot requires roughly ~40% equity (100 into a 150 pot) which 4‑5o won’t have against typical ranges. Raising here would be a thin, non+EV steal from UTG. Preserve the 10.9k stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

AQo is a premium hand from UTG+1 position in a 5-6 handed game. With UTG folded and no raises, standard play is to raise to isolate and build the pot. 3x BB (300) is a balanced size given stack depths (53BB effective) and opponents' tendencies to defend wide from blinds based on history (e.g., frequent calls/limps). Avoids limping which invites multiway pots with a strong but offsuit hand.

I am on the button with 7s 3c, which is a very weak starting hand. Elon has raised from UTG+1 to 300. While I have positional advantage, this hand does not have enough equity or playability to justify a call or a 3-bet against an opening range, especially one that has shown recent aggression. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack.

T9o is a marginal hand, especially out of position against an UTG+1 raise. While the price is reasonable (250 to call into 450), playing this hand OOP against an early position raiser with FiveTwo still behind is a -EV spot. Better to fold and wait for a stronger opportunity.

Elon is effectively opening from CO in a 5-handed game, so his range is wide enough that J4s is a reasonable BB defend. We’re getting a good price (200 to win 650 after calling), and the hand has decent playability via flush potential and some top-pair possibilities. A 3-bet bluff isn’t attractive here (poor blockers, OOP, and we don’t want to bloat the pot unnecessarily), so the best EV line is to flat and realize equity.

We’re OOP versus the preflop raiser on a fairly standard c-bet texture (Q-high with a wheel-ish low connect). With second pair (4x) and a weak kicker, our hand has some showdown value but doesn’t want to bloat the pot by leading into his range (which contains lots of Qx, overpairs, and good bluffs). Checking protects our range, lets Elon continue with his c-bets, and keeps the pot manageable so we can evaluate sizing (likely continue versus small bets, fold versus large pressure).

Top pair top kicker (Q-top) with A kicker on a draw-heavy flop (spade flush draw, 3-6 straight draw). As preflop raisor OOP vs a wide BB calling range (FiveTwo frequently defends wide per history, e.g., calls with marginal hands). Betting for value against weaker Qx (KQ, QJ, QT), floats, and weak pairs; also protects against draws. 450 is ~2/3 pot sizing: builds pot while denying equity to pure draws. FiveTwo checks behind weak holdings often, so bet extracts value and maintains aggression.

We have second pair (4x) on Qs-5s-4h with a weak kicker and no spade draw. Elon’s preflop raising range is wide and he’ll c-bet this texture at a high frequency, so folding would give up too much equity versus all his A/K highs and random overcards. Raising is unattractive because we don’t have strong blockers or a real draw, and versus his short effective stack he can often jam and force us off our marginal showdown value. Best line is to call once, keep the pot controlled, and re-evaluate on the turn (continue mostly when we improve to two pair/trips or when he slows down; fold versus big turn pressure on bad runouts).

Turn pairs the 5, giving us two pair (5s/4s) which is a clear upgrade, but it’s not invulnerable: any 5x now has trips and hands like 44/55/Q5/54 have us crushed. With Elon’s short-ish stack and aggressive tendencies, leading risks getting jammed on by a range that’s heavily weighted to better value, while also folding out a lot of his air that would continue bluffing. By checking we (1) keep his bluffs/spade draws and Qx barreling, (2) control pot size OOP with a medium-strong hand, and (3) can comfortably continue versus normal sizing while making better decisions versus extreme pressure.

Turn pairs the 5, giving the board two pair potential (Q5, 54) and trips (55), but my AQo remains top pair top kicker, ahead of most of FiveTwo's calling range which includes weaker Qx (KJ, JT, etc.), mid pairs, and missed draws from flop (spade flush draw, wheel/6-high straight draws). FiveTwo's flop check-call suggests capping strength—no sets likely on this board—and his turn check indicates weakness or trapping, but history shows passive play post-flop (frequent check-calls, rarely leading). Continue aggression OOP as preflop raiser to extract value from floats/one-pair hands and deny equity to live draws (remaining spades, runner-runner straights). 1000 is ~65% pot: balanced size building towards river shove if called, given pot will be ~2550 and my 4600 stack (effective ~3 streets left). Checking risks giving free cards to draws and loses value from his wide range.

We improved to two pair (5s and 4s) with a Q kicker, which is strong versus Elon’s wide CO-opening range and his flop/turn barreling range (lots of Qx, overpairs, and spade-draw bluffs). The problem is we’re drawing dead versus any 5x/boats, and if we raise here (especially with his short stack), we risk isolating ourselves against exactly those better hands while folding out the bluffs and thin value we want to keep in.

Given the price (1000 to win 3550 after calling) and his remaining ~1850 behind (likely to shove many rivers), the highest-EV line is to call and let him continue with dominated hands and missed spade draws. On the river, if he checks we can shove for value; if he jams, we’ll generally continue on blanks and be more cautious on runouts that heavily complete his value (e.g., spade/Q/5).

River pairs/three-spades runout makes our 5s5d+4h4d (two pair) a medium-strength bluff-catcher: we beat Qx/overpairs that slowed down, but we’re behind all 5x (trips+), boats (44/55/Q5/54), the newly-completed flushes, and the 63/A3 straights that got there on the 2s. Leading risks value-owning ourselves (getting jammed by better and folding out bluffs), while checking keeps Elon’s bluffing range in and also lets his sizing polarize his range so we can respond accordingly. Best play is to check and evaluate vs his bet size.

River 2s completes obvious spade flushes (board has three spades, so opponent needs two spades) and low straights (e.g., 63, 36), but my AQo remains TPTK, ahead of most of FiveTwo's check-call-call range: weaker Qx (QJ-QT, KQ unlikely OOP pre), overpairs that missed (88-JJ), mid pairs (77,66,44,33,22), and turned 5x or Ax high. His triple check induces bluffs but history (passive check-calls, rarely leading even with showdown value) suggests weakness or trapping rare. Preflop BB call + large check-calls cap his range—no sets/boats likely bet river. With pot 3550 and my stack 3600 (shove to pot), all-in is pure thin value: extracts from ~40% of his range (pot odds 3550:3600 ~50% to call into 7150, he calls worse ~30-40% equity needed, which he does per tendencies). Checking risks free showdown loss to draws/pairs or check-raise jam from nuts (unlikely). Max EV aggression OOP.

River is a very bad card for our hand: the 2s completes both the spade flush (any two spades) and the wheel straight (A3), and it also completes 63 for the 6-high straight. After we check/call flop and turn, Elon’s pot-sized all-in (3600 into 3550) is highly polarized.

Our two pair (5s/4s with J kicker) is near the bottom of the hands we arrive here with: we have no spade to block flushes, and we don’t block the main straight values (A3/63). We do block 44 (and some 54) which helps a little, but his value region is still very large: all 5x (trips+), all made flushes, and the newly-completed straights. Meanwhile, many of his natural bluffs from earlier streets (spade draws / A3 / 63) have now improved into value, so his bluffing density drops sharply on this runout.

Pot odds are 3600 to win 10750 (~33.5%), but given how much of his continuing range becomes value on this river and our lack of relevant blockers, we’re unlikely to realize that much equity. Best EV is to preserve our stack and fold.

5-handed game with deep stacks (71BB effective). 98d is a strong suited connector UTG in short-handed play, with excellent implied odds, straight/flush potential, and post-flop playability. Standard to open-raise for initiative, pot equity, and fold equity. Raising to 2.5x BB (250) is a balanced size to build the pot without overcommitting OOP while discouraging weak calls and setting up post-flop aggression. Folding is too tight; limping invites multi-way pots OOP.

I have Ace-King offsuit, which is a premium starting hand in a 5-handed game. Elon has raised from under-the-gun to 250. To maximize value and take control of the pot, I will 3-bet. A raise to 850 is a standard sizing that puts pressure on the opener and potentially isolates the hand. Given my stack size and position, I want to define the range now.
53 offsuit is a garbage hand. Facing a raise and a 3-bet, this is a clear fold. No playability, no implied odds worth chasing. Easy fold.

We’re in the small blind facing an UTG open and a strong-looking 3-bet from UTG+1. J6o plays very poorly out of position in a 3-bet pot: it’s frequently dominated when we make a pair, has weak straight potential, and we’ll be forced into lots of tough postflop spots with low realization. 4-betting as a bluff here is also poor given the positions and likely tight ranges. Best EV is to preserve the stack and fold.

Elon opened UTG to 250 and Flash 3-bet to 850. I’m in the big blind with pocket 2s (2h2c), pot 1250 and I must call 750 (total 850). I have ~108 BB and will act last postflop, so this is a classic set-mining spot: although flop-set odds (~11.8%) won’t justify the call on pure immediate pot odds, the deep stacks give good implied odds if I hit. Folding surrenders profitable equity; 4-betting or shoving with a small pair is overcommitting. Plan: call to see the flop (amount 850 total). If I hit a set I’ll extract value; if I miss I’ll keep the pot under control and fold to large, committed aggression (and be more cautious if it goes multiway since implied odds decline).

98d is a premium suited connector suited for calling 3-bets, especially in a 5-handed tournament spot with ~69BB effective stacks. Facing Flash's 3-bet to 850 (~3.4x over my UTG open) and Minni's cold-call from BB, the pot is 2000 and calling 600 commits me for a post-flop SPR of ~2.4 (6300 remaining into 2600 pot), which favors speculative hands like this with straight/flush potential and great multi-way playability. Equity realization is high vs polarized 3-bet ranges (AQ+/TT+) and loose calling ranges. Folding is too tight after opening UTG; 4-betting risks overcommitting OOP to likely calls/shoves while this hand prefers set-mining dynamics. Calling keeps pot controlled, builds it nicely, and positions for post-flop aggression (e.g., flush/straight draws, combo draws).

Preflop I called the 3‑bet to set‑mine with 2♣2♥. The flop is 5♠4♥4♠ — I completely missed. This is a 3‑way pot against the 3‑bettor (Flash) and the original raiser (Elon), both of whom have relatively strong ranges, so my pocket deuces have very little showdown value and almost no fold equity. I want to keep the pot small and avoid getting raised off a marginal hand. Action: check. Plan: fold to any significant bet/raise; I may call a very small probe bet (roughly ≤ ~1/3 pot, ~800–900) as a cheap attempt to hit a set, but otherwise preserve my stack and re‑evaluate on the turn. If I do hit a set later I will look to extract maximum value.

Flop is 5♠ 4♥ 4♠, a paired low board with flush draw potential (two spades). My 9♦8♦ has overcards (8 and 9), backdoor diamond flush draw, and backdoor straight draw (to 6-7 for 5-6-7-8-9), but no made hand or strong draw. Multiway pot (me, Flash with strong 3-bet range like JJ+/AK/AQ, Minni with loose flat-call range including suited connectors, pairs, Ax suited spades). Pot 2600, my stack 6300 (SPR ~2.4), low SPR favors controlled pot sizes. Minni checks first (weakness or trap), but betting here as first aggressor risks: 1) Flash raising with value (sets, two-pair, overpairs, spades draw); 2) Multiway calls bloating pot OOP to UTG+1; 3) Low fold equity vs ranges that flop well here (44, 54s, A4s, 45s, sets). Equity realization ~30-40% vs polarized ranges, but bluffing into two players suboptimal. Checking induces bets from Flash (likely c-bet), allows cheap fold/check or semi-bluff check-raise on scary turns (diamond, spade, 6/7/10), realizes backdoor equity cheaply, and pots control with float/showdown potential. Consistent with pre-flop plan for multiway speculative playability. Aggression better on turn/river with improved equity.

The flop is low and paired (5s 4h 4s), which doesn't interact well with my pre-flop 3-betting range. Both Minni and Elon checked to me. In a three-way pot, even as the aggressor, it's often wise to check back on boards that favor the callers' ranges (like medium pocket pairs). I have two overcards and the Ace of Spades for a backdoor flush draw. Checking allows me to control the pot size and realize my equity for free, while also seeing how the turn develops.

I called the 3‑bet preflop to set‑mine with 2♥2♣ but completely missed — board is 5♠‑4♥‑4♠‑3♣. This is a three‑way pot against players who showed strength preflop (Flash 3‑bet, Elon open), so my pocket deuces have almost no showdown value and very little fold equity. With a healthy stack (~101 BB) and the pot already at 2600 I want to keep the pot small and avoid getting raised off a marginal hand. I will check to control the size of the pot and preserve my stack. Plan: fold to any sizable bet or raise; I’ll only call a very small probe bet (roughly ≤ ~1/3 pot, ≈800–900) to try and hit the river. If I make a set on the river I’ll shift gears and extract value.

Turn brings the 3♣, completing low straight draws (A23, 25x) and gutshots (2x, 6x) while pairing lows further on a board of 5♠4♥4♠3♣. My 9♦8♦ remains a complete airball: no pair, no flush draw (backdoor diamonds only), no straight draw (98 too high for 3-4-5; needs 6-7-10+ for broadway). Equity vs. ranges ~20-30%: behind Flash's polarized 3-bet range (sets like 44/55, overpairs JJ+, TP like A4s/K5s, spade draws) and Minni's wide calling range (pairs, suited connectors, spades like A♠x/6♠x). Pot 2600, stack 6300 (SPR ~2.4), multiway low SPR favors checking marginal/no hands for pot control and cheap showdown/realization of overcards. Minni's second check signals weakness (missed draws/paired low, traps unlikely). Betting/raising as bluff suboptimal: low fold equity multiway (Flash protects value/spade draws, Minni floats loosely), bloats pot OOP to final SPR <1, commits stack with ~dead money. Checking induces Flash's c-bet bluffs/value thin, sets up cheap fold/call or check-raise semi-bluffs if river improves (diamond/spade/6/7/10). Consistent with flop check plan for speculative playability; preserves stack for better spots in tournament.

The turn is the 3 of clubs, making the board 5s 4h 4s 3c. Both Minni and Elon have checked to me again. My Ace-King high still has significant showdown value against their ranges, which likely consist of medium pocket pairs or broadway cards. By checking back again, I am exercising pot control and seeing a free river. Betting here is unlikely to fold out better hands like 66-99 and might lead to a difficult decision if I'm check-raised. I'm happy to take my equity to the river.

I called the 3‑bet preflop to set‑mine with 2♦2♣ but completely missed — the board on the river is 5♠‑4♥‑4♠‑3♣‑8♣. This was a three‑way pot against the opener and the 3‑bettor, both of whom carry strong ranges (overcards, mid/high pocket pairs, and the occasional set). My pocket deuces have almost no showdown value and very little fold equity multiway. Everyone checked down, so there’s no clear value to be gained by bluffing; a river stab would have poor chance of folding out better hands and would open me to a dangerous raise. With pot control being paramount, the prudent play is to check and take the showdown. If any opponent had fired a sizable bet, I would fold (only a very small probe bet would be considered for a call).

River 8♣ pairs my 8♦ for pair of 8s with 9♦ kicker (second pair on paired 4s board: 5♠4♥4♠3♣8♣). Provides thin showdown value vs. missed draws (suited connectors, spades/clubs that whiffed) and weak pairs in Minni's wide BB calling range, but crushed by Flash's polarized 3-bet range: overpairs JJ+, sets (44 quads, 55/33/88 two-pair), trips (A5s/A4s/A8s/53s etc.), any 44/Ax low. All-street check-check-check-check history signals caution (value slowplayed unlikely multiway OOP; more likely air/floats). Minni's leading river check weakness. Low SPR (~2.4, 6300 into 2600) favors pot control. Betting thin value suboptimal: low fold equity multiway (Flash protects, Minni stations), risks bloat/raise by better hands, denies my own showdown equity. Checking: realizes equity safely, free showdown vs. Minni air, induces Flash thin value bets/call-downs profitably with blocker to str8s/gutshots, preserves shorter stack (6300) for tournament spots. Consistent with preflop speculative 98d plan (playability > protection).

The board has run out 5s-4h-4s-3c-8c. After 3-betting pre-flop and having both opponents check to me on the flop and turn, I have checked back to exercise pot control and realize my equity. On the river, both Minni and Elon have checked again. My Ace-King high has reasonable showdown value in this spot, as it beats all non-pairing broadway hands (like AQ, AJ, or KQ). While a pair of 66 or 77 would beat me, it is unlikely that a bet would reliably fold out such hands after I have shown weakness by checking twice. By checking behind, I can reach showdown for free and potentially win the pot against missed hands, while avoiding the risk of a check-raise or being called by a better hand that was simply playing cautiously.

Ten-Four offsuit is a very weak starting hand, especially from Under the Gun (UTG) position. With four players left to act behind me, there is a high probability of facing a better hand or a raise. There is no strategic reason to enter the pot with such a poor hand in this position.
54 offsuit in UTG+1 is a clear fold. This is a very weak hand and I'm in early position with players yet to act behind me. No reason to invest chips here.

Folds to us on the button 3-handed, so we should be applying max positional pressure with a wide open. 78o isn’t premium but it’s connected and plays well in position, and raising is clearly higher EV than limping since we can win the blinds frequently and still realize equity when called. I’ll use a small, standard open to keep risk low while still forcing the blinds to defend/3-bet appropriately.

I’m in the small blind with TT facing a button open to 250 (pot 400, I need +200). Stacks are deep (~100bb). TT is a strong value hand vs a BTN open and I prefer to 3-bet for value/isolation rather than call out of position and play a large post‑flop pot. I’ll raise to 1000 total (a ~4x open) — big enough to fold out weaker broadways/Ax and discourage a multiway pot from the BB, but sized to get called by worse pairs and some suited broadways. Plan: if called I’ll proceed cautiously OOP (c-bet favorable flops, control pot on dry boards); if I face a sizable polarized 4-bet I’ll heavily discount my hand and likely fold unless the sizing or player reads justify a call.

Facing a button raise to 250 and a small blind 3-bet to 1000 preflop from the BB with 4c9c. This is a weak suited connector with significant gaps. While it has some playability for flush and straight draws, the 3-bet indicates strength from Minni, and FiveTwo's button range is wide but could include strong hands. Pot odds require calling 900 into 1350 (about 2.5:1), needing ~29% equity, but out of position multiway against likely strong ranges, equity is insufficient. Folding preserves stack for better spots in this tournament with 110 hands left.

We opened the BTN and face a small-ish SB 3-bet to 1000. With 7♠8♥ (connected, decent equity vs a likely wide SB 3-bet range) and position in a heads-up pot, calling is higher EV than folding, while 4-bet bluffing this offsuit combo is too low-quality. We’re getting ~2.8:1 immediate price and can realize equity well in position by continuing mainly on favorable textures (straight/2-pair potential) while being cautious on marginal one-pair 7x/8x holdings that are often dominated.

I 3‑bet preflop to 1000 for value/isolation and FiveTwo called. On the flop (9♦ A♥ K♣) I’m first to act out of the small blind with TT. This is a poor c‑bet spot: two overcards (A,K) hit a large portion of the caller’s continuing range (Ax, Kx, broadways) and I’m OOP, so leading will often get me called or raised by better hands. I’ll check to control the pot and to avoid bloating the pot with a vulnerable pair. Plan: check-call small sizing (≈ ≤1/3–1/2 pot) to find out if this is a bluff or probe; fold to large polarized bets unless I pick up a clear read or improvement on the turn; if FiveTwo checks back I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn and try to realize equity cheaply or apply pressure on safe runouts. Checking keeps options and protects most of my stack here.

SB is the preflop 3-bettor and this A-K-9 rainbow board heavily favors their range (lots of Ax/AK/KQ/TT-QQ that connect). With 7♠8♥ we have very little immediate equity and a flop stab is unlikely to generate enough folds versus a range that can comfortably continue. Taking the free card in position preserves stack, realizes our small backdoor straight potential, and lets us bluff more credibly on specific turns (T/6/5) if checked to again.

Check. I 3‑bet preflop and both of us checked the A♥ K♣ 9♦ flop to control the pot; the turn J♣ is a hostile card — it pairs a lot of hands (Jx) and completes more Broadway combos — and I’m OOP with only pocket tens. I currently have only a pair of tens with 6 clean outs to improve (four Qs to make a Broadway straight, two 10s to make trips) — roughly ~12–14% to hit the river — so I don’t want to inflate the pot when I’m likely behind to Ax/Kx/Jx. There’s also no realistic club flush for me on the river (I have one club, two clubs on board, only one card left). My plan is to control the pot by checking, realize my showdown equity, and then react to any river action: call reasonable thin bets, fold to large polarized barrels, or consider a bluff on a very safe river runout if villain shows weakness. For now: check (amount 0).

Minni (the preflop 3-bettor) has now checked twice on an A-K-9-J board. While this runout still favors their range, the double-check caps them a bit (lots of missed broadways/medium pairs that don’t want to bloat the pot), and in position we can profitably apply pressure.

With 7♠8♥ we have a gutshot to the T, but not enough equity to just hope to get there; taking a small stab can win the pot immediately often enough, and it also lets us potentially barrel selectively if they call and then check river. I’ll use a smaller sizing to keep risk down and deny equity to random floats, and we comfortably fold if they check-raise (their value region dominates us).

Call 750. FiveTwo’s tiny turn probe (750 into a 2850 pot) is consistent with a wide, thin/value and blocking range — missed broadways, Jx, some Ax/Kx thin bets and occasional bluffs. I have an underpair (TT) with ~6 relatively clean outs to trips/straight (~12–14% to hit the river) and calling 750 gets me ~17% pot odds; plus my pair is still ahead of a fair share of the blocker/missed hands. Raising OOP here would be too risky and folding surrenders too much equity for a very small price. Plan: call to see the river, fold to a large polarized river bet, call small river bets and freely value-call/raise if I improve to trips/straight.

The river (J♠) pairs the jack and makes my pocket tens a very weak underpair vs FiveTwo’s calling/turn‑betting range. He cold‑called my 3‑bet preflop and then led a small probe on the turn (750 into the ~2850 pot) — lines consistent with Ax, Kx, Jx, medium pairs and some bluffs. Any Jx (or Ax/Kx) now beats me and my hand has almost no fold equity, so betting would be a thin, negative‑EV bluff. I’ll take the free showdown: check to avoid bloating the pot with a likely second‑best hand. If he fires a small river bet I can consider a call for pot odds; I’ll fold to a large polarized river shove.

We have 7-high and essentially zero showdown value after our turn stab gets called. Minni’s line (check flop, check/call turn, check river) is capped a lot of the time at one-pair hands (Ax that doesn’t want to bloat the pot, Kx, QQ–TT, some floats) that are now under real pressure on A-K-9-J-J.

The river pairing the J is a strong card to bluff because our turn bet can credibly represent Jx that improved on the turn and is now trip jacks / boats, and those value hands would often size up here for value. A bigger, polar sizing maximizes fold equity versus exactly the hands we’re targeting, while we can comfortably fold if check-raised (their raise range is heavily value).

Call 2900. Board: 9♦ A♥ K♣ J♣ J♠; I hold TT in the small blind. Pot is 6500 and FiveTwo’s river bet of 2900 gives me ~30.9% pot odds (2900/(6500+2900)). He cold‑called my 3‑bet preflop, both checked the flop, then he made a small turn probe (750) and now a ~44%‑pot river stab. That line is not a highly polarized shove — it frequently contains one‑pair hands (Ax, Kx), thin Jx value, and a mix of missed broadways/bluffs. Many combos beat me (AJ, KJ, Kx/Ax, sets), but there are enough worse hands and bluffs in his range that my underpair has reasonable showdown equity against the range weighted by his sizing. Raising would be a poor bluff: I have very little fold equity and would be committing a large portion of my stack out of position. Folding surrenders too much equity for a medium-sized bet. Calling 2900 realizes my equity, preserves stack flexibility (leaves ~5475 behind) and is the highest EV play here.
T7o is a weak hand, especially from UTG position. This is a clear fold - not enough equity or playability to open raise from early position.

5-handed from UTG+1 with T2o is far too weak to open: it’s heavily dominated, plays poorly postflop, and doesn’t realize equity well out of position versus the blinds and later positions. With ~60bb and no pressure to gamble, the highest-EV line is to avoid entering the pot.

Button with Ah9c is a clear open-raise spot — strong high-card, good blocker to Aces, and huge positional advantage against the blinds. With UTG and UTG+1 folded I want to isolate the blinds and take the initiative; a standard 3x BB open (to 300 total) applies pressure while keeping the pot manageable vs these stack depths. Plan is to c-bet favorable flops and use position; fold to a large, polarized 3-bet/shove but consider calling smaller 3-bets to realize position equity.

AQo is a premium hand from the small blind facing a button raise. Minni's button raising range is likely wide, including many hands I dominate like KJo+, suited broadways, Ax suited, suited connectors. Folding is too tight; calling is passive and allows multiway pots or BB squeezes. 3-betting to 900 (3x her raise) isolates the button, takes initiative, builds the pot with my equity advantage, and pressures her wide range. My stack of 88BB supports this aggression. Postflop playability is strong if called. Flash BB may fold or call light, but range favors my hand.

I am in the Big Blind with A5o. The action has been a raise to 3BB from Minni on the Button and a 3-bet to 9BB from Elon in the Small Blind. A5o is a weak hand to call a 3-bet with, especially being out of position against two active players. The Ace blocker is useful, but the hand is frequently dominated by Elon's 3-betting range (AQ, AJ, AT, etc.) and lacks post-flop playability in a bloated pot. Given the stack depths and the strength shown by the SB 3-bet, folding is the most disciplined and profitable long-term decision.

I call to 900 total. On the button with Ah9c I opened to 300; Elon's 3‑bet to 900 from the SB is common and I prefer to realize position equity rather than 4‑bet light. A9o has a useful Ace blocker and good post‑flop playability; calling 600 into a 1300 pot (~24% equity needed) is justified vs a typical SB 3‑bet range. I keep the pot controllable, will c‑bet favorable flops (A‑ or dry boards) and fold to large, polarized 4‑bet/5‑bet shoves; a 4‑bet here would be too thin with this kicker.

Preflop 3-bet with AQo from SB vs Button open is standard aggression, isolating her wide range while building pot with equity. Flop 8s8c3h is extremely dry and paired, hitting very few combinations in Minni's 3-bet calling range (primarily QQ+, AKo/s, maybe JJ/TT). She likely has overpairs or AK/AQ most of the time, which are crushed by my perceived range including AA/KK/sets/88. Heads-up, first to act, pot 1900, my 7900 stack. Strong c-bet for value from her folds and thin value from worse like AK. Half-pot sizing ~950 pressures without overcommitting. Blocks AA/AK improve my equity if called. Checking is too weak and invites bluffs/check-raises.

Elon’s flop bet is small (~1/3 pot). On 8s 8c 3h I have Ace-high (Ah9c) — no pair but an Ace blocker and a backdoor heart. Pot odds to call: I must put 950 into a ~3800 pot (950/(3800+950) ≈ 20%), so I need ≈20% equity. Against a SB 3‑bett c‑bet range this hand has enough equity vs bluffs/weak pairs and it’s cheap to realize position. Raising to 1900+ would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and commit me against many hands that currently dominate me (TT+, sets). Plan: call the 950, keep the pot controllable, and re‑evaluate on the turn — look to fold to large aggression if I miss, or use position to apply pressure if the turn helps (A/9/heart) or villain shows weakness.

Turn card 6h is mostly a blank but completes heart flush draws. Board remains paired and low, favoring my strong 3-bet range (sets, overpairs). However, AQo is pure air: ace-high, no pair, no flush draw (only backdoor), no straight potential. Minni's flop call on 8s8c3h suggests overpair (QQ-JJ/TT), AK/AQ, or rarely better. Her range crushes me; equity <15% vs that. Double-barrel bluff into stationish tendencies (from history: calls down multi-street in pots like H25, H34, H40) has low fold equity and commits to large pot (~50% stack if called). Checking pot-controls, induces bluffs/value from weaker Ax/draws, realizes any thin equity if miraculously ahead. Fold to bet. Preserves 69BB stack for premium spots in tournament.

Elon checked the turn after a small flop c-bet — a sign of weakness or pot control. I hold Ah9c: no pair but I block many Ax combos and have position. My earlier plan was to use position to apply pressure when the SB shows weakness. The board (8s 8c 3h 6h) is static — there’s no realistic draw I’m drawing to — so this is a pure bluff-try to take down the pot now. I’ll bet about half-pot to maximize fold equity vs medium-strength hands (overcards, single-pair underpairs, many missed c-bet combos) while keeping the pot manageable if called. Bet 1,900 into 3,800 is large enough to force folds from lots of bluffs and marginal pairs but small enough that I can fold to significant aggression; if Elon raises I’ll fold (I have no pair), and if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the river (only continue with a pair or if the river gives me extra equity).

Facing Minni's turn bet of 1900 into 5700 pot after my check with AdQh (ace-high, backdoor flush draw only). Preflop 3-bet range is strong (QQ+, AK/AQ), flop c-bet on paired 8s8c3h dry board hit that range hard; her call suggests overpair (QQ-TT/JJ), AK/AQ, rarely sets. Turn 6h is a blank for my hand but adds flush draws/straight possibilities. My hand has minimal equity (~10-20% vs value range), poor implied odds (5050 behind into 12.6k pot post-call, SPR <0.5). Minni's history shows station-like tendencies: heavy multi-street calls (H2, H9, H15, H34, H40), few bluffs. Low fold equity on raise; calling risks stack without showdown value or strong draws. Pot odds require ~25% equity (1900/7600), which I lack vs polarized range weighted to value. Fold preserves 6950 stack (69BB) for better spots in tournament phase.

5-handed UTG is still out of position versus three players, and 9-7 offsuit is too weak/undominated to profitably open into opponents who have shown willingness to 3-bet and apply pressure. Limping is also unattractive (invites iso, plays poorly multiway). With 60bb and a shorter stack relative to others, preserving equity and waiting for better openers is higher EV.

Five-handed, 168BB deep — Jc8c is a playable, suited hand with good postflop potential. Folding is too tight and limping hands like this from UTG+1 surrenders initiative and allows cheap multiway pots. I want to take the initiative and apply pressure to the blinds, so I’ll open-raise to 300 (3x BB). This sizing is standard for short-handed play: it gives fold equity, discourages marginal defenders, and keeps the pot manageable versus the deep stacks behind. Plan: if I get single-call I’ll c-bet favorable textures and pursue club/straight equity; if I face a large 3-bet I’ll usually fold and preserve my deep stack (I can continue only against small 3-bets or with reads).

J5s is a speculative suited hand with a gap, but from the button facing an early position raise from Minni (UTG+1). Minni's range is likely strong, and my hand has poor equity against it (dominated if they have Jx, low playability if called). Pot odds are 450:300 (~1.5:1), not enough for the ~35-40% equity needed postflop. Position is good, but better to fold and wait for stronger hands or steals later. Stack is ~70BB, no need to speculate early in tournament (hand 42/150). Preserve stack for value.

I am in the Small Blind with 9c 2c. Minni raised from UTG+1 to 300. 9-2 suited is a very weak hand, and calling puts me out of position against a strong early-position range. There is no reason to defend this hand here.
9-4 offsuit is a very weak hand with poor playability. Even getting ~2.25:1 pot odds, this hand plays terribly post-flop against a raising range. Easy fold from the big blind.

UTG with 2♦8♦ offsuit — a complete junk hand. I'm first to act with four players yet to act, two of whom have big stacks and can easily 3-bet or isolate. Calling 100 into a 150 pot to see a flop with a hand that's dominated by almost every calling/raising range is a losing long-term play, and opening or limping here is unnecessary risk with deep stacks. Standard strategy is to fold and wait for a much better spot in position or with a real hand.

5-handed play with stacks around 60-170BB. A2s is a strong suited ace from UTG+1 (second to act after UTG fold), with excellent playability: nut flush potential, wheel straight potential, and ace blocker. Standard to open raise rather than limp or fold. Opening size of 3x BB (300) is appropriate given pot of 150 and typical sizing from history (250-300 common). Builds pot, takes initiative, and balances range. FiveTwo in BB has shown aggression but stack covered. Postflop potential justifies the play for long-term stack maximization.

I am on the button with 7-5 offsuit. Elon has opened for 300 from early position. 7-5 offsuit is a very weak hand that does not play well against an opening range from that position, even with the benefit of being on the button. I have a healthy stack and there is no reason to get involved in this pot with such a marginal hand.

T9o is a playable suited connector-type hand. Facing a standard 3x open from Elon in UTG+1, I'm getting decent pot odds (250 to win 450). The hand has good straight potential and I can outflop weaker parts of his range. Calling to see a flop is reasonable, though I'm out of position. Folding is also fine but T9o has enough playability to continue.

8♥2♣ is among the weakest holdings and plays terribly out of position, especially multiway versus an UTG+1 open and a small blind cold-call. While the pot odds (200 to win ~900 after calling) look tempting, the hand realizes equity very poorly and is prone to reverse implied odds when we make a weak pair. A squeeze bluff has no blockers and risks running into strong ranges, so the highest-EV line is to simply release.

Flopped top two pair (tens and nines) on a 9c-3h-Td board. This is a monster hand. I should bet for value - Elon raised preflop so he likely has overcards or pocket pairs that will call. Leading out makes sense since this board connects with my SB calling range. Betting around 60% pot to build the pot and get value from his continuing range.

Preflop raise with A2s was standard for its playability, suited ace with nut flush draw potential and wheel straight potential, plus ace blocker. Flop 9c-3h-Td is a dry, rainbow board with no direct draws hitting strongly for my range. I have position on Claude (SB caller pre), ace-high with backdoor spade flush draw and backdoor wheel straight draw (A-2 with 3 on board). Claude's donk-lead of 450 into ~700 pot (~65% pot) is often strong (Tx, 9x, sets) but SB cold-call ranges include floats, Ax suited/weak aces, suited connectors. Ace blocker reduces his Ax. Pot odds 450 to call into 1150 (~3.6:1) require ~22% equity, which A-high has decent vs his range (~30-40% equity likely as often best hand now). Calling maintains balance, controls pot size OOP opponent's lead, realizes equity with position for turn, avoids bloating pot with marginal hand. Raising risks overcommitting without strong equity/fold equity on dry board; folding too tight after seizing initiative pre. Plan: call flop, evaluate turn board texture and Claude's action (likely check-back weak or bet strong). Deep stacks (100BB+) favor pot control and postflop playability.

I have top two pair on a 9c-3h-Td-4c board. The 4c is a complete brick. Elon called my flop bet, suggesting he has a pair, overcards, or a draw. With this strong hand, I need to continue building the pot and extracting value. Betting around 65% pot (~1000) keeps his calling range wide while building a pot where I can get stacks in on the river.

Preflop A2s raise standard for playability. Flop call correct with position, ace-high equity (~30-40% vs polarized donk range), ace blocker reducing opponent's Ax, and implied odds deep. Turn 4c changes little for me—no improvement, still ace-high with minimal backdoors (spade flush, wheel gutshot unlikely). Board now has two clubs (9c-4c), potentially completing club flush draws or runner-runner straights, but favors Claude's likely Tx/9x/set/pair+draw range from repeated OOP aggression (flop donk, turn bet ~half-pot into 1650). Pot odds 1000 into 2600 (~3.6:1, 22% needed), but equity <22% now vs strengthened range (he continues betting, less bluffs). Raising bluff poor: dryish board, his range strong, fold equity low OOP; minraise to 2000 commits too much without equity. Calling risks river overbet/shove OOP strength; preserves 6200 stack (93BB) for better spots in tournament (hand 43/150, 5 players). Folding disciplined, avoids spew, maintains image as tight UTG raiser.

K6s is a marginal hand from UTG in a 5-player game. Standard UTG opening range is tighter: pairs 66+, AJo+, KQo+, suited aces, broadway suited connectors. K6s has poor playability multiway, dominated by better kings, and reverse dominated by Ax suited. With aggressive opponents like Minni, Claude, and FiveTwo behind (evident from history of frequent raises and 3-bets), raising risks isolation or multiway pots OOP. My 62BB stack doesn't necessitate widening range. Limping is exploitable and poor. Pot odds don't justify calling. Folding preserves stack for better spots over 150 hands.

I am in UTG+1 in a 5-handed game, which is effectively the Hijack position. J8s is a marginal but playable opening hand in this configuration. Based on my previous play, a standard open of 3x (300) has been effective at picking up blinds or establishing control. While Claude is on the button and likely to play back, J8s has decent post-flop playability. If I encounter a significant 3-bet from the remaining players, I can comfortably fold without sacrificing too much of my stack.
As Th on the button is a strong hand facing a UTG+1 open. I have position and a good stack. 3-betting here isolates Flash and takes control of the pot. Standard 3-bet sizing to ~3x their raise.

Facing a raise (300) and a button 3-bet to 900, KJo is too often dominated by Claude’s value-heavy 3-bet range (AK/AQ/KQ/KJ+, big pairs), and calling puts us out of position in a 3-bet pot with poor realizability—plus the BB can come along cheaply, making our life even harder. A 4-bet bluff is also unattractive with our 59bb stack: it risks bloating the pot OOP versus a player who can continue in position and/or 5-bet, and our blockers (one king, one jack) are not strong enough to justify it. Best EV is to preserve the stack and wait for a better spot.

I’m in the big blind with Q♦2♣ facing an UTG+1 open (300) and a button 3-bet to 900. To call is 800 into a 1,350 pot. Q2o is a very weak, poorly playable hand here — it’s frequently dominated by the 3-bettor’s range and plays poorly multi-way if Flash elects to come along. A light 4-bet bluff from the BB would be thin and ill-advised with this holding; calling 8bb just to see a flop commits me OOP into a bloated pot with little equity or postflop maneuverability. With deep stacks there will be plenty of better spots to invest chips. Fold and preserve my stack.

I opened with J8s from the Hijack, which is a standard loose-aggressive open. However, Claude's 3-bet to 900 from the Button puts me in a difficult spot. Playing J8s out of position against a 3-bet is generally unprofitable, as the hand doesn't have enough equity against a typical 3-betting range and I will be at a positional disadvantage for the remainder of the hand. Although Claude is an active player, I prefer to fold here and preserve my stack for a more advantageous situation rather than calling 600 more with a marginal hand.

I am in the UTG position (under the gun) in a 5-handed game, which is equivalent to the Hijack position. My hand, Ace-Seven offsuit (Ad 7s), is a marginal holding in this spot. Looking at the table dynamics, Claude (UTG+1) has been playing very aggressively, 3-betting me in previous hands (Hand #44) and showing strong post-flop aggression. Minni is also a very active big stack. Given that A7o plays poorly against a 3-bet and is difficult to play out of position against aggressive opponents, a fold is the most disciplined and conservative play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.
A8o in UTG+1 is a playable hand. With a healthy stack of 190BB, I should open raise to 2.5x to build a pot and isolate. Standard opening from early position.

Button vs Claude’s 2.5bb open, K2 suited is a playable defend in position in a 5-handed game. 3-betting here would require an unrealistically high immediate fold rate given the pot size, and getting called/4-bet by a big-stack opener puts us in tougher, higher-variance spots with a dominated king. Calling keeps ranges wide, realizes our suited equity (flush/straight possibilities), and lets us leverage position postflop while controlling pot size vs a capable, aggressive opponent and two players behind who can still squeeze.

Claude opened UTG+1 to 2.5BB and the Button called, so this will be a multiway pot and I'll be out of position versus both. K♥J♠ is too strong to muck against an early raise but not strong enough to 3-bet for value here (3-betting risks a squeeze from the BB and won’t get great fold equity with the button calling). I’ll call the extra 200 (250 total commitment) to see a flop and keep the pot manageable. Postflop plan: play cautiously OOP — continue with top pair or strong draws, fold to heavy aggression without improvement, and look to realize equity or pick good spots to bluff if the action simplifies.

In the big blind facing a min-raise from early position (Claude UTG+1) and calls from the button (FiveTwo) and small blind (Minni), the pot is 850 and I only need to call an additional 150 (total bet 250). This gives excellent pot odds of about 5.7:1. KTo offsuit is a decent speculative hand with king high, straight potential (broadway), and domination over weaker kings or tens in calling ranges. Multiway pot favors implied odds for hitting top pair top kicker or draws. Folding is too tight given the price, and raising offsuit KTo OOP against an early raiser and callers risks too much stack (effective ~5600) without great postflop prospects. Calling keeps the pot multiway cheaply, realizes equity well, and maintains flexibility postflop where I act first but with deep stacks relative to the pot.

Flop A♥ 9♥ 8♥, I hold K♥ J♠ in the SB — I have no pair but the nut heart draw (9 outs: ~19% to hit on the turn, ~35% by the river). This is a multiway pot (Claude opened, Button and BB called) and I’m out of position, so my fold equity is very limited. Betting as a semi-bluff here would bloat the pot against multiple callers and invite raises from strong Ax/overpair holdings; that’s not a spot I want to build the pot out of position. I’ll check to control the pot and realize equity. Plan: if checked to and a heart hits I’ll bet/raise for value; if I miss the turn I’ll be willing to call small bets to see the river but will fold to heavy aggression without improvement.

The flop of Ah9h8h is a nightmare for my KTo offsuit: no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw, just king-high with minimal showdown value in a multiway pot. The board is extremely draw-heavy with flush draws, open-enders (e.g., 10-7, J-10, 7-6), and likely hits villains' ranges (Claude's UTG raise could include Ax, broadway; callers have speculative hands that connect). Minni checked from SB, but acting OOP first postflop multiway with nothing, betting (donk) risks raising villains' strong hands or draws getting value/collusion. Checking is mandatory: realizes no equity cheaply, induces bluffs/checks from missed hands, and allows folding to aggression on turn/river without cost. Pot odds irrelevant since no bet to call. Goal is stack preservation in tournament; avoid spew.

Flopped two pair (Aces and Eights) on a very wet 8h9hAh board with three hearts. Strong hand but extremely vulnerable - any heart makes a flush, JT makes a straight. With 3 opponents still in, I need to bet for value and protection. Betting around 65-70% pot to charge draws.

Monotone Ah-9h-8h flop in a 4-way pot is a very poor spot to continue with Kc2c (no heart). We have essentially no real equity: no made hand, no heart to represent/nut-block, and only a thin backdoor club-flush chance plus some weak pairing outs that are often dominated by any ace/heart. Claude’s ~40% pot c-bet into multiple players will get continued against frequently (any heart, any ace, many pairs/straight draws), so raising as a bluff is low-EV and calling is burning chips with little chance to realize. Best play is to preserve stack and fold.

I have K♥J♠ on A♥9♥8♥ — I don’t have a made hand but I have the nut single-heart draw (9 outs: ~19% to hit the turn, ~35% by the river). Claude (UTG+1) opened and now bets 700 into a 1700 pot — a relatively small c‑bet. This is a multiway pot and I’m out of position, so my fold equity is limited; 3-betting as a semi-bluff would bloat the pot and risk a shove/re‑raise from Claude. Calling is +EV here: required equity to call is ~700/(1700+2*700) ≈ 22.6% and my ~35% by the river exceeds that. I’ll call 700 to realize my equity, see the turn, and fold to heavy turn aggression if I don’t improve; if a heart or a favorable card hits I’ll re-evaluate for value/raise.

Facing a 700 bet into 2400 on a highly coordinated Ah9h8h flop after checking with KdTc offsuit (king-high, no pair, no draws), the hand has zero equity against Claude's strong UTG continuation range (Axh, sets, two-pair) or Minni's calling range (draws, floats, weaker Ax). Pot odds of ~3.4:1 are insufficient for king-high in a multiway spot with reverse implied odds; villains likely have Aces, flush draws, or straights. Raising lacks fold equity given their commitments and board texture. Folding preserves stack for better spots in tournament with 59BB effective depth, avoiding spew on a flop that demolishes my speculative calling range.

I hold K♥J♠ on 8♥-9♥-A♥-K♠. On the flop I had the nut heart draw and called Claude’s small c‑bet; the turn pairs my King so I now have top pair plus the nut heart draw — a very strong two‑way hand heads‑up. Claude’s flop bet was small and represents a wide range (Ax, Kx, flushes, bluffs), so I want to both get value from worse Kx/medium pairs and charge any turn/river cards that could improve him while denying a free river. I’ll lead for value/protection with a ~half‑to‑two‑thirds pot bet to extract from worse hands and narrow his range. If he raises large I’ll respect stronger made hands (flush/trips/Ax) and re‑evaluate (likely folding to an overcommitment, calling or flatting smaller raises to realize equity).

I have two pair (Aces and Eights) on 8h9hAh Ks. Minni check-called my flop bet then leads 1500 on turn. This line is concerning - could be a made flush, AK that improved, A9 two pair, or a semi-bluff with hearts. My hand is strong but vulnerable. The Ks didn't complete the flush, so that's good. Getting ~2.6:1 odds. I should call to control pot size - raising risks getting jammed on by better hands while folding is too weak with two pair. I'll evaluate river.

Recap: I turned top pair (K♥J♠) after raising the turn to 1,500 and Claude called. The river (4♣) is a brick that doesn’t complete any extra relevant draws. Claude opened UTG+1 and c‑bet the heart‑heavy flop, then called my turn raise — his calling range here contains lots of A‑x (some of which beat me), made heart flushes (if he held two hearts), sets/9s/8s, and some Kx / weaker pairs. Betting the river would risk getting value‑called too often by hands that beat me (A, flushes, sets) and will make many better hands call while worse hands fold — I’d be bloating the pot out of position with a marginal ONE‑pair hand. Checking controls the pot, lets me realize showdown value versus worse Kx/medium pairs, and gives Claude a chance to bluff missed heart draws. I’ll check the river and see a showdown (and be prepared to call a reasonable small/medium river bet but fold to a large shove that polarizes toward flush/Ax).

Minni led turn for 1500 then checked river on the 4c brick. This line suggests they don't have a made flush or straight - those hands would bet for value. Their check indicates either a weaker Ax hand, a busted draw, or possibly A9/AK trying to get to showdown. My two pair (Aces and Eights) beats most hands that would check here. I should bet for value targeting worse Ax hands - around 40-50% pot to get called by medium strength hands.

I will call 2200. I turned top pair (K♥) after being on the nut-heart draw and raised the turn for value/protection; Claude called. The river 4♣ is a blank and I checked to control the pot per plan. Claude’s 2200 into a 7600 pot gives ~22% pot odds (2200/(7600+2200) ≈ 22.4%). His line (UTG+1 open, small flop c-bet, call of my turn raise) includes A‑x, some made flushes/sets, but also Kx, weaker pairs and occasional bluffs. My K♥J♠ has enough equity versus that mixed range to justify a call, and 2200 is small relative to my ~14.4k stack. I’ll call to see showdown and fold to any large shove that polarizes his range further.
7c8h UTG is a weak hand. While 78o has some playability as a connector, it's not strong enough to open from UTG in a 6-handed game. I should fold and wait for a better spot.

With 5-handed action and no raise yet, Q6o from UTG+1 is too weak and dominated by the continuing ranges of the Button and both blinds (many better Qx and broadways). Limping creates tough multiway spots with poor playability and reverse implied odds, and opening risks getting 3-bet or called by stronger hands. Preserving ~56bb and waiting for higher EV opens is best.

I’m on the button with 2♥6♣ — a very weak hand with little showdown value. With only the small blind (Elon) and big blind (Flash) left to act, this is a classic spot to attempt a button steal rather than limp and invite multiway action where I’ll be dominated. I’ll open-raise to 300 (a modest 3x BB) to generate fold equity and leverage position. If I face a 3-bet I’ll fold — 2-6o has poor equity and isn’t worth getting involved out of position or bloating the pot. If called, I’ll c-bet appropriate flops and fold to strong resistance. This size is big enough to pressure the blinds but keeps the pot manageable given my hand.

54s is a marginal hand at best, suited connector but very low. Facing a button raise from Minni, who has been active in recent hands. I'm OOP post-flop as SB, pot odds are about 1.8:1 effective (250 to call into 700), but multiway potential with BB behind, and hand plays poorly OOP without initiative. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots in tournament with 59BB effective.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten of Spades and Five of Hearts. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While I am getting decent pot odds to call (200 to win a pot of 450), T5o is an extremely weak hand that performs poorly out of position. Given that there are 150 hands in this tournament and I am currently middle-of-the-pack in stack size, there is no reason to defend with junk hands. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

5-handed UTG is effectively a middle position open. J8s has decent playability (suited, can make strong draws/straights) and benefits from initiative. Limping invites multiway vs bigger stacks (Claude/Flash) and puts us in tougher spots; folding is a bit tight this short-handed. I’ll open-raise to a standard size to pressure the blinds and keep ranges wider, while still allowing a comfortable fold if faced with a large 3-bet.

Pocket nines (9♦9♠) UTG+1 vs an UTG open to 250 — a hand I want to play aggressively. 99 is strong enough to 3-bet: it has good equity vs an opener's wide-ish UTG range and benefits from isolating the opener and avoiding multiway pots. Stacks are deep-ish (effective vs the opener ≈ 5.4k), so a standard 3-bet size that creates fold equity but doesn't invite huge squeezes is appropriate. Raising to 1000 (about 4x the open) balances value and fold equity: it folds out many dominated broadway and suited hands, gets value from worse pairs, and leaves room to fold to a large 4-bet shove if necessary. If I'm called, I'll proceed postflop as the 3-bettor; if I face a substantial 4-bet, I will re-evaluate (likely fold to an over-aggressive shove from a deeper player).

J2o is a speculative trash hand with no high card strength or playability. Facing an UTG raise and 3bet from UTG+1, opponents' ranges are strong (premium hands like QQ+, AK). Calling 1000 into 1400 pot offers poor odds for a multiway pot where I'll be OOP postflop against tight ranges. No fold equity with a shove given stack sizes and positions. Folding preserves my 59BB stack for better spots in a long tournament (47/150 hands).

I am holding 2s 4d in the small blind, which is an extremely weak starting hand. There has already been an open-raise from FiveTwo and a 3-bet from Minni, indicating very strong ranges from early position. There is absolutely no reason to enter this pot with such a poor hand and out of position. Folding is the only viable play.
Facing a 3-bet to 1000 with 7s8h offsuit in the BB. This is a weak hand against Minni's 3-bet range. Even with pot odds of about 1.5:1, 78o plays poorly OOP against a 3-bet range. Easy fold.

Minni’s 3-bet to 1000 puts us in a low SPR spot if we call (pot ~2150, ~4400 behind, SPR ~2) and we’ll be out of position. J8s (even suited) is too dominated versus a reasonable 3-bet range and doesn’t realize equity well with this stack depth. 4-betting would be largely a bluff and too expensive with only 54bb. Best decision is to cut losses and preserve stack.

Holding Td6c UTG in a 5-handed game with ~128bb effective, this is a marginal offsuit hand that is often dominated and plays poorly out of position. Calling 100 (limping) will likely produce multiway pots with little fold equity; a light open (or a min-raise to 200) from UTG is too speculative and will often be called or 3-bet by players who have shown aggression. With both shorter stacks (FiveTwo, Elon) and a very deep, aggressive stack (Claude) behind, preserving chips and waiting for a stronger, more playable spot is higher EV. Fold.

A2 offsuit is a very weak hand from UTG+1 position in a 5-handed game. No steals or limps before me (Minni folded UTG), so standard play is to fold preflop. Avoid playing out of position with a dominated hand that has poor postflop playability. Preserve stack for premium hands.

I am on the button with 9s7c. Both players ahead of me have folded. While this is an opportunity to steal the blinds, 97 offsuit is quite weak, even for a button opening range. Claude (SB) has a massive stack and has shown a willingness to be aggressive, and FiveTwo (BB) is short-stacked enough to potentially shove over a steal attempt. Given the tournament context and my current stack size, I will choose a disciplined fold and wait for a hand with better playability or higher cards.

Folded to me in the SB with T3 suited hearts. This is a good spot to attempt a steal against the BB. T3s has some playability with flush potential if called. Standard raise to 250 to try to take it down preflop.

Claude is opening from the small blind in a short-handed (5-handed) spot, so his range should be wide. From the big blind I’m getting a good price (150 to win 350, ~30% required), and J8o has enough equity versus a typical SB opening range to defend. A 3-bet bluff isn’t attractive with this offsuit, dominated-prone hand and limited playability out of position, so I’ll realize equity by calling and playing postflop.

I have T3hh on a 4h-7s-5d board. I only have a backdoor flush draw and an overcard. This connected low board hits a lot of BB's calling range (small pairs, connectors like 68, 86, 34, etc.). With such a weak holding out of position, a c-bet is unlikely to get many folds and I risk getting check-raised. Better to check and see a free card - I have 9 outs if a heart comes on the turn for a flush draw.

Claude’s SB opening range is wide, and this 4-7-5 flop interacts well with my BB defend range (more 2-pair/straight combos like 76/54/64). After he checks, his range is often overcards or marginal pairs that will fold to pressure. With J8o I have a gutshot to the 6 plus some backdoor potential, so a small stab is a good semi-bluff to win the pot immediately and still have equity when called. If he check-raises, I’ll just fold this hand.

I have T3hh with only a backdoor flush draw (one heart on board) and an overcard. FiveTwo's small bet of 225 into 500 pot gives me ~3.2:1 odds, but my equity is weak (~15-18%) with just backdoor draws. This low connected board (4-5-7) hits BB's calling range well - pairs, straight draws like 68 or 86. Out of position with minimal equity and no real draws, calling hoping to catch a heart on the turn is speculative. Disciplined fold here.

UTG with 2c Ts, a weak offsuit hand with no connectivity, high card value, or suitedness. Early position requires a tight range to avoid playing out of position post-flop against 4 opponents. Folding preserves stack for better spots; no steal incentive given multiple players to act behind and solid stacks.

I am in UTG+1 in a 5-handed game with Ace-Four suited (A4d). With Elon (UTG) folding, I am the first to act. A4s is a strong starting hand in short-handed play, offering nut-flush potential and good playability. Raising is the standard move here to take initiative and build a pot or win the blinds. I'll open for 300 (3x BB).

KQs on the button is a strong hand. Flash opened to 300 from UTG+1. This is a great spot to 3-bet for value with position. I'll raise to 900 to isolate and build the pot with a premium suited broadway hand.

We’re in the small blind facing an open (300) and a 3-bet to 900 from the button, with the big blind still behind. A6o is dominated by both players’ value-heavy ranges, plays poorly out of position in a bloated pot, and we have no good continue vs a 5-bet jam. Cold-calling invites the BB to squeeze and leaves us in tough postflop spots, while a 4-bet bluff is too high-variance and low-EV with A6o (unsuited, weak playability). Best decision is to conserve chips and fold.

Facing an open to 300 (Flash UTG+1) and a button 3-bet to 900. Pot ~1350 and I must call 800. I hold Q♥6♣ — a weak, poorly connected offsuit hand that is frequently dominated and plays very badly out of position in a 3-bet pot. Calling 800 to play OOP against two aggressive players is high variance with little equity; 3-betting as a bluff is too risky with this holding. With a healthy stack and many hands left, preserving chips for better spots is the correct long-term play. Fold.

I opened the pot with Ace-Four suited, a strong speculative hand in short-handed play. Claude responded with a 3-bet from the Button. Given the pot odds (600 to call into a total pot of 1350) and the post-flop playability of nut-flush potential and wheel draws, calling is the most standard and effective play. 4-betting into the chip leader is unnecessarily risky, and folding would be too exploitable given the hand's strength.

On this flop of Ten-Four-Jack with two diamonds, I have bottom pair (Fours) and the nut flush draw with my Ace of diamonds. Given that Claude was the pre-flop 3-bettor and has the positional advantage, the standard play is to check to the aggressor. My hand has significant equity against his likely range of high cards and overpairs, so I am very comfortable seeing a bet from him and continuing in the hand.

Excellent flop for my KdQd. I have a royal flush draw (9 diamond outs), plus 3 non-diamond Aces for broadway - roughly 12 outs. Flash checked to me. With this strong semi-bluff opportunity, I should bet for value and protection. Betting around 65% pot to charge draws and potentially take it down now.

On a flop of Td 4c Jd, I have bottom pair (Fours) and the Nut Flush Draw (Ad4d). Claude, the pre-flop 3-bettor and current chip leader, has made a continuation bet of 1,250 into a pot of approximately 1,950. While I am out of position, my hand has significant equity against almost his entire range; I have 9 outs for the nut flush, 2 outs for trips, and potentially 3 outs for two pair (Aces), totaling about 14 outs if he holds an overpair like KK or QQ. Calling is the most prudent play here: it keeps the pot manageable, allows me to realize my equity, and keeps his range wide, including his bluffs or weaker high-card hands. Raising would likely only get called by hands that dominate my pair or force a shove, which I'd be inclined to call but would rather see a turn card first to see if I improve.

On the turn, the Jack of Hearts pairs the board. I currently hold bottom pair (Fours) with the Nut Flush Draw. As I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor and current chip leader, checking is the most prudent action. This allows me to see Claude's reaction to the board pairing. If he checks behind, I get a free card to try and hit my flush or an Ace.
